title: SASS variables in compiled CSS output with 5.3.0-alpha1
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

A handful of SASS variables seem to be sneaking through into the compiled CSS with Bootstrap 5.3.0-alpha1. This is a regression from 5.2.3.

I'm compiling Bootstrap using webpack, with an unmodified npm install of Bootstrap 5.3.0-alpha1 and the latest versions of node-sass (8.0.0) and sass-loader (13.2.0).

Am I missing something here? Is there a requirement change in the intended way of compiling SASS for Bootstrap 5.3.0-alpha1? Or are these calc declarations with SASS variables not being properly interpolated in Bootstrap source? e.g. `#{$var}`

----

# Example 1

For example, take the `.table-group-divider` rule in the file `_tables.scss` in 5.2.3 vs. 5.3.0-alpha1:

### 5.2.3

#### _tables.scss line 44:

```
.table-group-divider {
  border-top: ($table-border-width * 2) solid $table-group-separator-color;
}
```

#### Compiled output:

```
.table-group-divider { border-top: 2px solid currentcolor; }
```

### 5.3.0-alpha1

#### _tables.scss line 44:

```
.table-group-divider {
  border-top: calc($table-border-width * 2) solid $table-group-separator-color; // stylelint-disable-line function-disallowed-list
}
```

#### Compiled output:

```
.table-group-divider { border-top: calc($table-border-width * 2) solid currentcolor; }
```

Note that **$table-border-width** incorrectly appears in the compiled output in 5.3.0-alpha1, which results is an invalid CSS rule.

----

# Example 2

Similarly, a variety of `$input-border-width` instances also incorrectly end up in the compiled CSS due to the change in how `$input-height-border` is defined in `_variables.scss`:

### 5.2.3

#### _variables.scss line 850:

```
$input-height-border:                   $input-border-width * 2 !default;
```

### 5.3.0-alpha1

#### _variables.scss line 890:

```
$input-height-border:                   calc($input-border-width * 2) !default; // stylelint-disable-line function-disallowed-list
```

### Compiled output (in a variety of places):

```
min-height: calc(1.5em + 1rem + calc($input-border-width * 2));
```

### Reduced test cases

N/A

### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Windows

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Chrome

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

v5.3.0-alpha1

title: Color values should use a global css variable instead a fixed hex color
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

Theming bootstrap via sass is "easy" for a fixed theme and colors, but if you want your website be color-reactive or customized per user it become unreliable

E.g. When you want to customize your primary color you set it as:
`$primary: #429bf5`

Then you compile the sass and now all components will have the value #429bf5 in places where the primary color should be, if you want all the components use a different color you have to set them individually, would be better if bootstrap generates some css global like `--bs-theme-primary:  #429bf5` for the primary color and then in every component just use var(--bs-theme-primary) to get that color and then we can just change that value to affect all the components

### Motivation and context

- The color in a global scope will be easy to customize even in a separate css file if you don't want to customize via sass, at least colors...
- Increases flexibility to customize and more css variables always are welcome
- Avoid changing every hex value or attribute per component, just one change and that's all

title: Add Vertical Breakpoints
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

As with the existing horizontal breakpoints, define three default vertical breakpoints.

| Breakpoint | Class Infix | Dimensions |
|-------------|------------|---------------|
|  Small        | *None*     | <416px     |
|  Medium   |   `vm`        | ≥416px   |
|  Large       |    `vl`          | ≥720px   |

Then update the actual breakpoint naming to be `-{Horoziontal Breakpoint}-{Vertical Breakpoint}`

i.e. for Sticky Top, you would end up with the following examples

- `sticky-top` (No Break Points)
- `sticky-vm-top` (Only Vertical Break Point)
- `sticky-vl-top` (Only Vertical Break Point)
- `sticky-sm-top` (Only Horizontal Break Point)
- `sticky-sm-vm-top` (Both Breakpoints)
- `sticky-sm-vl-top` (Both Breakpoints)
- etc.

Further, either `{media-}breakpoint-*` mixins and functions should accept vertical breakpoints or an equivalent set of mixins and functions should be provided. ,

### Motivation and context

I came up with this idea while working on a website and examining how it would appear on a smartphone orientated in the landscape. I found that the 64px sticky navbar caused problems in this layout, and my solution, in this case, was to use the following custom CSS:

```css
@media (min-height: 416px) {
  .stick-vm-top {
    position: sticky;
    top: 0;
    z-index: 1020;
  }
}
```
This works fine for this single-use case, but my first solution was to check the breakpoints in bootstrap, which is when I realised that bootstrap doesn't currently have any vertical breakpoints. So, I needed to make the sticky top breakpoint class manually.

Having realised this lack, I can think of several other used cases, such as the display classes, to hide redented or unnecessary content when vertical space is limited or only show it when there is a lot of vertical space.

Also, imposing image height restrictions when viewport height is limited.
author: glenfletcher

title: Bootstrap v5.1.x causes 10x performance decrease.
body:
We recently upgraded our application from Bootstrap v4.6.2 to v5.2.3 and immediately noticed a severe rendering/"Total Blocking Time" performance degradation. Oddly enough, our application only makes light use of Bootstrap.

Here are the numbers for each Bootstrap version:
v4.6.2: Total Blocking Time: 232ms
v5.0.2: Total Blocking Time: 385ms
v5.1.3: Total Blocking Time: 2744ms
v5.2.3: Total Blocking Time: 2872ms
v5.3.0-alpha1: Total Blocking Time: 3024ms

The Total Blocking Time is almost entirely taken up by Rendering in the profiler due to a large number of forced reflows compared to older versions.

I should also mention these times also do not include any load times, this is simply after our single-page application is fully loaded and launching an edit screen with some input fields on it. 

Any ideas what would be causing this when going from v5.0.2 to v5.1.3?

title: link color dark should use $primary like non-dark links do
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

regular links work great when changing the primary color:

```scss
$link-color: $primary !default;
$link-hover-color: shift-color($link-color, $link-shade-percentage) !default;
```

however, when in dark mode, they always default to blue:

```scss
$link-color-dark: $blue-300 !default;
$link-hover-color-dark: $blue-200 !default;
```

i propose they use `$primary`, like regular links:

```scss
$link-color-dark: $primary !default;
$link-hover-color-dark: lighten($primary, 10%) !default;
```


### Motivation and context

normally when i change the primary color i want that reflected in all elements of the UI like it does in light mode.

in dark mode, the links are hard coded to blue by default.
author: jerothor

title: MultiSelect For Bootstrap 5 similar to select 2 (Floating Labels, Ajax, Data source, Pagination)
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

Dear development team,

I'm writing to request a new feature for Bootstrap 5: a MultiSelect component similar to Select2. While there are some third-party libraries available, it would be great to have a multi select component built directly into Bootstrap, as it would ensure consistency with the rest of the framework and simplify development for those of us who are already using Bootstrap.

Specifically, I'd love to see a MultiSelect component that includes the following features:

Floating Labels: the ability to use floating labels for the input field, as well as support for different label positions (e.g. above, below, inside).
Ajax Support: the ability to load options asynchronously via AJAX, with support for pagination and custom filtering.
Data Source: the ability to use a data source (e.g. an array or JSON object) to populate the options, with support for nested data structures and custom templates for the options.
Pagination: the ability to paginate through large data sets, with support for both server-side and client-side pagination.
I believe that a MultiSelect component with these features would be a valuable addition to the Bootstrap framework, and would be a popular choice for developers who are looking for a flexible and powerful way to handle multiple selections in their forms.

Thank you for considering my request, and please let me know if you have any questions or need any additional information.

Best regards,

Owais.multi select

### Motivation and context

I'm writing to request a new feature for Bootstrap 5: a MultiSelect component similar to Select2. While there are some third-party libraries available, it would be great to have a multi-select component built directly into Bootstrap, as it would ensure consistency with the rest of the framework and simplify development for those of us who are already using Bootstrap.

title: Add a pricing component with toggle switch to Bootstrap
body:
### Prerequisites - [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests - [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Proposal The pricing component with toggle switch would be built with default Bootstrap components and utilities with little customization. It would consist of a row of cards that show the name, price, and features of each plan. The cards would have a border and background color that match the primary color of Bootstrap. The most popular or recommended plan could have a badge or a different color to stand out from the rest. Above the cards, there would be a toggle switch that changes the prices from monthly to yearly or vice versa. The prices would be updated dynamically using JavaScript. Some examples of websites that use pricing components with toggle switch are: [Spotify] [Netflix] [Dropbox] ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/77841233/226450187-7ba6746c-e1d4-4a5c-9bb4-3780aa51a2f6.png) `

Choose your plan

Free
$0 / mo
  • 10 users included
  • 2 GB of storage
  • Email support
  • Help center access
` ### Motivation and context The motivation for adding a pricing component with toggle switch to Bootstrap is to provide a simple and effective way for users to display different pricing plans for their products or services and let customers choose between monthly and yearly billing options. This feature would enhance the usability and functionality of Bootstrap as a framework for building responsive websites that offer subscription-based services or products with different tiers of features or benefits. The context for adding this feature is that many websites nowadays use pricing components with toggle switch to show their customers the different options and prices they have for their services or products. This feature is especially useful for websites that offer subscription-based services or products with different tiers of features or benefits, such as music streaming, video streaming, cloud storage, etc. By using a pricing component with toggle switch, users can easily compare the different plans and choose the one that suits their needs and budget. Moreover, by allowing customers to switch between monthly and yearly billing options, users can also increase their conversion rate and customer loyalty by offering discounts or incentives for longer-term subscriptions.

title: Add a Timer Picker component to Bootstrap
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

 **A time picker component** is a user interface element that allows users to select a specific time of day. It typically appears as a pop-up modal or a drop-down menu with options for selecting the hour, minute, and possibly AM/PM format.
Time pickers are commonly used in web applications for tasks such as setting the start or end time of an event or appointment, selecting a time range for filtering data, or setting a reminder or alarm. 

![68747470733a2f2f692e6962622e636f2f425a30566e79622f696d6167652e706e67](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/118993199/226772981-4652e30c-46a3-44fd-a32b-5288c42e6037.png)

**In this example**, you can select the time with the hour and the minute and you can also select the AM/PM format also!






### Motivation and context

**Time pickers** are a common user interface element, Adding a time picker component to Bootstrap could make it more versatile and adaptable to a wider range of design needs, and provide developers with a new tool for creating more sophisticated and functional web applications. Time pickers can be implemented using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.
A time picker component can provide users with a more convenient and intuitive way to interact with time-based data, Without a time picker, users may have to manually enter time values, which can be time-consuming and error-prone. A time picker component can help streamline this process and make it more user-friendly.

title: Build error in `carousel.scss` when using `$color-mode-type: media-query`
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

When I add `$color-mode-type: media-query;` to my app with Bootstrap v5.3.0-alpha2 I get the following error:

```
| Error: Top-level selectors may not contain the parent selector "&".
|     ╷
| 233 │ ┌     .carousel,
| 234 │ │     &.carousel {
|     │ └───────────────^
|     ╵
|   node_modules/bootstrap/scss/_carousel.scss 233:5          @content
|   node_modules/bootstrap/scss/mixins/_color-mode.scss 12:9  color-mode()
|   node_modules/bootstrap/scss/_carousel.scss 232:3          @import
|   bootstrap/scss/bootstrap.scss 42:9                        @import
|   app/assets/stylesheets/application.bootstrap.scss 9:9     root stylesheet
```

This change in `carousel.scss` was introduced by #38209 and when I revert the change locally I can build the CSS of my app and the dark mode works.

### Reduced test cases

See here: https://github.com/vinc/runa.sh/blob/558b9df43203f6744a76e8b90166dc50bdea8312/app/assets/stylesheets/application.bootstrap.scss#L7

Fix: remove line 234 of `carousel.scss` (but this could break what #38209 was trying to fix)

### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Linux

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Firefox

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

v5.3.0-alpha2

title: Responsive Offcanvas component not reflecting the value of $offcanvas-bg
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

Changing the `$offcanvas-bg` variable does not reflect on a responsive Offcanvas component when in an above-the-breakpoint mode. That is, if the `$offcanvas-bg` variable is set to `#00f` then an `*-sm` component will appear blue after sliding out on a mobile device but will be transparent on desktop.

### Reduced test cases

It can be seen here https://codepen.io/SpTm/pen/xxaBKbN

The reason for this is the code at https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/5975ca65c552184889df25b8cd1c62e3a58ad621/scss/_offcanvas.scss#L100, which I'm guessing needs to replicate the code from line 43 instead:
> background-color: var(--#{$prefix}offcanvas-bg);

I can do the PR if you think this is the right solution to the problem. (And if you too see it as a problem ;) )

Thanks a lot!

### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

macOS

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Chrome

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

5.2.3

body:
### Prerequisites - [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues - [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems - [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Describe the issue I am using the `media-query` color mode type so that auto dark/light mode can work without JavaScript. The colors appear correct when the browser is set to dark mode, but when the browser is set to light mode the brand and link color in the navbar becomes dark, which makes it very hard to read against the background: ![Screenshot in light mode](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3053271/228362187-918d95ce-a501-4480-83aa-f58aa92078c5.png) I am able to work around this by using the `navbar-dark` class instead of setting the `data-bs-theme="dark"` attribute, but the documentation says this class is deprecated, so I expected it to work with the recommended attribute. ### Reduced test cases ```html
My Brand
Home About
``` ### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on? Windows ### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on? Chrome, Firefox, Microsoft Edge ### What version of Bootstrap are you using? v5.3.0-alpha2

body: ### Prerequisites - [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests - [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Proposal Re-introduce static popovers and tooltips like in Bootstrap 3: https://getbootstrap.com/docs/3.4/javascript/#static-tooltip https://getbootstrap.com/docs/3.4/javascript/#static-popover ### Motivation and context See https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues/14439 > so that anybody can inspect the properties of Tooltip [and Popovers] Currently you cannot inspect (using the DevTools) a tooltip or a popover HTML & CSS: the element hides as soon as you click anywhere Also it would allow to use a tooltip or a popover without JavaScript/Popper.js/Floating UI

title: Scrollspy and French anchor links
body:
### Prerequisites - [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues - [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems - [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Describe the issue Scrollspy behavior has changed between the v5.1 and the v5.2 regarding the handling of characters in anchor links/ids. It was possible before to do the following: ```html
Navbar
  • Présentation

Second heading

``` Now, using an id like "présentation" throws an error in the console: > Uncaught DOMException: Element.querySelector: '#pr%C3%A9sentation' is not a valid selector It is linked to https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues/37545 and other issues in the past. Different opinions have been raised in comments on these issues and maybe not have been studied because the issues were already closed. Maybe we can reconsider it and provide a final answer based on more recent data. I don't know what they use but for example, Wikipedia has such ids when the page is in French (e.g. https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bootstrap_(framework)#G%C3%A9n%C3%A9ralit%C3%A9s). https://www.w3.org/TR/CSS21/syndata.html#value-def-identifier was mentioned as an answer but is more linked to CSS isn't it? But if I create the following, its works: ```css #présentation { color: red } ``` https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/dom.html#the-id-attribute seems more permissive and more linked to this issue considering only HTML. --- /cc @Lausselloic ### Reduced test cases - In v5.1: https://codepen.io/julien-deramond/pen/ExeJWEo - In v5.2: https://codepen.io/julien-deramond/pen/OJoGpOK ### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on? Windows, macOS, Android, iOS, Linux ### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on? Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Microsoft Edge, Opera ### What version of Bootstrap are you using? v5.2

title: Copy button not consistently visible on Edge browser when window size is reduce
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

I've noticed that when reducing the size of the browser window in the Edge browser, the copy button for code snippets in Bootstrap does not always appear. Sometimes it's visible, but other times it's hidden and requires scrolling to become visible.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/77841233/228798723-82d45ba6-c5ca-4c22-bfc1-7125110f469f.png)

The copy button should always be visible on the page, regardless of the window size or device used to access the page.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/77841233/228798798-e2ad8ce1-6ded-4b0a-b21a-494cee446b9d.png)

The copy button is not consistently visible on the page when reducing the size of the Edge browser window, making it difficult for users to copy code snippets.

### Reduced test cases

The addition of a media query that particularly targets Edge and makes sure the button is always displayed at all window widths is one potential fix. Another option would be to change the button's CSS to increase its adaptability to various screen widths.



### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Windows

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Microsoft Edge

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

v5.3(alpha)

title: Tooltip not disposed after ajax request
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

When using tooltips on elements that cause a postback using ajax, tooltips do not hide and do not get disposed, leaving the tooltips in the DOM. Because I'm using ajax, I need to invoke the tooltip init script on every request causing for duplicate tooltip objects while the old ones lay around in the screen. There were some fixes for versions <=4 of BS but didn't find any for BS 5.3.

### Reduced test cases

As you can see, the tooltip remains in DOM after pressing that green button that causes an ajax request to load data and open a window.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8672899/229205904-b946886c-b6c0-4bc0-b206-5da60a74d6a7.png)

I'm using this code in a function to initialize tooltips:

`const tooltipTriggerList = document.querySelectorAll('[data-bs-toggle="tooltip"]');
 const tooltipList = Array.from(tooltipTriggerList).map(tooltipTriggerEl => new bootstrap.Tooltip(tooltipTriggerEl));`

What can I do to fix this issue?  

For now I'm manually cleaning the dom before initializing but it would be nice to have this controlled out of the box.

```
// Get all the tooltip elements that may exist in DOM and remove them
 const tooltipElements = document.querySelectorAll('.tooltip[role="tooltip"]');

// Loop through each tooltip element and remove it from the DOM
tooltipElements.forEach((tooltipElement) => {
      tooltipElement.remove();
});
```

### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Windows

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Microsoft Edge

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

5.3.0.alpha2

title: Dropdown opens and re-closes with one click in Firefox when emulating mobile device
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

When clicking on a dropdown menu in quick succession, clicks after the first seem to trigger both a show and hide event, so the menu ends up in the same state (either shown or hidden) as when I clicked. I've provided a minimal CodePen example below and included a GIF of the behavior exhibiting.

This happens in Firefox when emulating a mobile device. I used Galaxy S20 Android 11. It does not occur when using Firefox at a normal resolution with no mobile emulation enabled. It does not occur in Chrome with or without emulation.

In this GIF, I click a few times slowly to demonstrate the menu opening and closing as expected. I then click somewhat quickly, and the menu frequently opens and re-closes or closes and re-opens in response to a single click.
![JTapG4k](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6531664/230179987-313a33f1-1902-4dca-a56f-09c552709eab.gif)

### Reduced test cases

Minimal reproduction example. A menu with a single dropdown element.
https://codepen.io/ryanwawr/pen/gOBOYWd?editors=1111

Windows

Firefox

5.2.3 in CodePen. Also exhibits in a project I'm developing that uses 5.1.0.

title: Reusable Sass utilities API documentation section
body:
Enhancement suggested in https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/pull/38219#discussion_r1148250058.

Our "Sass utilities API" sections are always written the same way.

We would need a shortcode or partial or anything else with two params:
- First part of the sentence (e.g. "Background" or "Object fit")
- The param (the key) used for `scss-docs` (e.g. "utils-bg-color")

We would like to call it like this:

```
{{< sass-utilities-api-section "Background" "utils-bg-color" >}}
```

And it would contain something like:

```
### Sass utilities API

{{ firstParam }} utilities are declared in our utilities API in `scss/_utilities.scss`. [Learn how to use the utilities API.]({{< docsref "/utilities/api#using-the-api" >}})

{{< scss-docs name="{{ secondParam }}" file="scss/_utilities.scss" >}}
```

We would need some help to do it. On my side, I don't exactly how to build it with Hugo. I tried a few things but haven't managed to succeed by calling `scss-docs` and `docsref` within another shortcode or from a partial.

author: ZhangChengLin

title: Offcanvas with `backdrop: false` should have `aria-modal="false"`
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

An offcanvas with `backdrop: false`, thus without backdrop, should have `aria-modal="false"` instead of `aria-modal="true"`. Indeed, without backdrop, the content underneath the offcanvas is accessible and can be focusable.

See [`aria-modal` MDN Web Doc](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Accessibility/ARIA/Attributes/aria-modal#:~:text=Modal%20dialogs%20are,the%20modal%20content.) for further info:

> Modal dialogs are when content is displayed and the user's interaction is limited to only that section until it is dismissed.
> 
> When creating modal dialogs, `aria-modal="true"` tells assistive technologies that the windows underneath the current dialog are not part of the modal content.

---

The line to be fixed is the one below, there should be a condition:

https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/137bfbad7bc41025be94fddf739e24a645e6c119/js/src/offcanvas.js#L111

---

Summary table:

| `backdrop` | Current `aria-modal` | Expected `aria-modal` | Is correct? |
| :--------: | :------------------: | :-------------------: | :---------: |
| `"static"` |        `true`        |        `true`         |     ✔️      |
|   `true`   |        `true`        |        `true`         |     ✔️      |
|   `false`   |        `true`        |        `false`        |     ❌      |

### Reduced test cases

CodePen: https://codepen.io/Elysiome/pen/abRvaWY

### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Windows

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Chrome

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

v5.2.3

The styling of selected options for multiple selects appears out-of-family as the highlight is restricted to the `<option>` tag, inside padding of the parent `<select>`. See the CodePen for comparison to possible fixes, which have been verified in multiple browsers:
* **Poor**: As-is
* **Good**: Move horizontal padding from `<select>` to `<option>`
* **Better**: Move all padding from `<select>` to `<option>`

title: Bootstrap hard codes the assumption that hover/active states will be shaded in light mode and tinted in dark mode
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

Bootstrap hard codes the assumption that hover/active states will be shaded in light mode and tinted in dark mode. 

This can be seen in the button variant mixin's definition: 

```
@mixin button-variant(
  $background,
  $border,
  $color: color-contrast($background),
  $hover-background: if($color == $color-contrast-light, shade-color($background, $btn-hover-bg-shade-amount), tint-color($background, $btn-hover-bg-tint-amount)),
  $hover-border: if($color == $color-contrast-light, shade-color($border, $btn-hover-border-shade-amount), tint-color($border, $btn-hover-border-tint-amount)),
  $hover-color: color-contrast($hover-background),
  $active-background: if($color == $color-contrast-light, shade-color($background, $btn-active-bg-shade-amount), tint-color($background, $btn-active-bg-tint-amount)),
  $active-border: if($color == $color-contrast-light, shade-color($border, $btn-active-border-shade-amount), tint-color($border, $btn-active-border-tint-amount)),
  $active-color: color-contrast($active-background),
  $disabled-background: $background,
  $disabled-border: $border,
  $disabled-color: color-contrast($disabled-background)
)
``` 
This makes it difficult to lighten a component's background instead of darkening it on interaction. The choice to shade or tint a component in a given context should be a configurable option and not a hard coded assumption.

Tinting on interaction:
![Screenshot from 2023-04-12 18-19-45](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/56766/231606896-a258c8ea-6b9a-492f-bf12-b69e9b5c2b9f.png)

Achieved with: 

```
@each $color, $value in $theme-colors {
  .btn-#{$color} {
    @include button-variant(
      $value,
      $value,
      $color: color-contrast($value),
      $hover-color: color-contrast($value),
      $hover-background: tint-color($value, $btn-hover-bg-tint-amount),
      $hover-border: tint-color($value, $btn-hover-border-tint-amount),
      $active-color: color-contrast($value),
      $active-background: tint-color($value, $btn-active-bg-tint-amount),
      $active-border: $value
    );
  }
}
```

Over the default of shading on interaction:
![Screenshot from 2023-04-12 18-20-25](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/56766/231606906-fc3e205e-3d26-4e17-a7e1-8e791a436a31.png)

Potential pitfalls:

Contrast. The return value of `color-contrast` is dependent on the background value and can change the color of the text from white to black when lightening. This isn't much of a concern as the user is already interacting with the component and the tint/shade intensity can be configured by the dev.

### Reduced test cases

N/A

### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Windows, macOS, Android, iOS, Linux

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Microsoft Edge, Opera

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

v5.3.0
author: michaelurban

title: Issue with Hardcoded Height of Form-Controls in Bootstrap 5's Floating Labels
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

Hello,
I noticed that in Bootstrap 5, the form-control heights are hardcoded when using floating labels. Specifically, the following CSS lines are responsible:

```
.form-floating>.form-control, .form-floating>.form-control-plaintext, .form-floating>.form-select {
    height: calc(3.5rem + var(--bs-border-width)*2);
    line-height: 1.25;
}
textarea.form-control {
    min-height: calc(1.5em + 0.75rem + var(--bs-border-width)*2);
}
```

These lines prevent us from setting the height of a textarea using the rows attribute, as we would normally do. Instead, we are forced to define the height for each text area individually, which is not ideal.

I would suggest considering a modification to these CSS rules to allow the use of the rows attribute for setting the height of Textareas wherever possible, as this is a more convenient and intuitive method.

This isn't as urgent but I just enclosed this issue so you can release a fix in upcoming version of bootstrap.

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

### Reduced test cases

rows="" attribute doesn't work on textarea when inside bootstrap floating labels. We need to manually specify the height of each texatrea which is inconvenient.

### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Windows, macOS, Android, iOS, Linux

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Microsoft Edge, Opera

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

5.3

title: Extra margin between <legend> and <div class="row g-3">
body:
### Prerequisites - [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues - [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems - [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Describe the issue I don't really expect a fix for this because this will go away when bootstrap switches from gutters to gaps (see #37384). Still I wanted to document this issue so others can find it. Sometimes multiple input elements should be grouped together, e.g. a date and a time input or 3 select elements that represent day, month, and year (these may not be perfect examples, but you get the idea). Visually it makes sense to put these inputs in a row. Semantically it makes sense to put these inputs in a `
`. Now the trouble is: `` does not work well with negative margins that are used to implement vertical gutters (tested in firefox and chromium). This is an issue with the implementation of gutters, so using gaps instead solves this issue. Fortunately, gaps work for flex elements in all browsers, so we can use `class="g-0 gap-3"` as a workaround. ### Reduced test cases ```html
Date time
``` ![screenshot](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/202576/232898291-55027470-453a-4216-92c7-b377160d367d.png) ### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on? Linux ### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on? Firefox ### What version of Bootstrap are you using? 5.2.3

title: Insufficient contrast on borders
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

[WCAG 2.1](https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG21/#non-text-contrast) requires a contrast of 3:1 for non-text content. Many elements, e.g. form inputs or checkboxes, mostly consist of borders, at least in some of their states. It is therefore important that the border color has sufficient contrast to the background.

[`#888888`](https://contrast-ratio.com/#%23999-on-white) would have sufficient contrast to white. Bootstrap 5.2 uses [`#ced4da`](https://contrast-ratio.com/#%23ced4da-on-white), which only has a contrast of 1.49 to white. Bootstrap 5.3 takes this even further by using [`#dee2e6`](https://contrast-ratio.com/#%23dee2e6-on-white) which only has a contrast of 1.3 to white.

I do not have any vision impairments that I know of, but to me these borders are practically invisible (depending on the lighting conditions). I strongly recommend to revert at least to the level of Bootstrap 5.2.

Bootstrap 5.3 introduces variables for the border color, so a workaround would be to set `--bs-border-color: #888888`.

### Reduced test cases

![#888888](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/202576/232973631-a415ac4e-611b-4064-b23e-b36daa5d525f.png)
![Bootstrap 5.2.3](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/202576/232973636-9a4e3ef4-bd48-46df-8479-d3e1e5dfd8a3.png)
![Bootstrap 5.3.0-alpha3](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/202576/232973635-274cc544-2c0f-48a1-90db-7efc05dd1a84.png)


### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Linux

5.3.0-alpha3

author: ZhangChengLin

title: Bootstrap UI Library - Reducing Unused CSS and JS
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal
Bootstrap is a widely used UI library for web development, but it comes with a large size of CSS and JS files, including many features that may not be necessary for all users. For example, the library includes multiple color variations for tables, buttons, spinners, progress bars, placeholders, navbars, list groups, cards, and alerts, but users typically use only one or two colors.


#### Problem:
Problem:
The size of Bootstrap's CSS and JS files is too large and may cause slower loading times and increased page weight. Additionally, including unnecessary features can make the library more complicated to use and require more customization.

#### Proposal:
- Reduce the number of color variations and components included in Bootstrap.
- Only include the most commonly used components, such as buttons, forms, and typography.
- Allow users to customize their own color scheme or choose from a few pre-defined options.
- Create a lightweight version of Bootstrap with only the most essential components.


### Motivation and context

#### Context:
- Bootstrap library is very large in size due to various color variations and components.
- Most users only need one or two colors and only use a few components.

#### Motivation:
- Smaller file sizes will improve website load times and performance.
- A simplified version of Bootstrap will be easier for developers to use and navigate.
- More customization options will allow developers to create unique designs while still using the Bootstrap framework.
- Improving user experience will lead to more satisfied users and increased usage of Bootstrap.

title: Memory leak with dynamic html + All components
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

Tooltip (popover) on dynamically added html elements are not getting cleared from the ElementMap after removing that html. If we reinsert the html content, elementMap is getting created with new entries and old element entries never gets removed.

### Reduced test cases

1. Open https://vishnutk.github.io/bootstrap/
2. Open browser console in debugger
3. Click on `Template Toggle` button
4. Mouseover on `Template Dynamic Button 1`
5. Watch for the console log
6. Click on `Template Toggle` button to remove html
7. Mouseover on `Template Dynamic Button 1`
8. Watch for the console log

Everytime the new entry gets added in the ElementMap.

Reason: elementMap.has is checking by reference but we are getting new element.

```
const Data = {
            set(element, key, instance) {
              if (!elementMap.has(element)) {
                elementMap.set(element, new Map());
              }

              const instanceMap = elementMap.get(element);
```

### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

macOS

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Chrome, Firefox

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

v5.2.3

title: Sanitizer doesn't allow sip in href. Sip links are used for messaging like in Microsoft Teams.
body: ### Prerequisites - [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests - [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Proposal I wasn't sure if this would be considered a bug or feature request. The sanitizer doesn't recognize sip as a valid type of url. https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/1fcae4393c9a723fffa5d8e923ab9f2050ac6273/js/src/util/sanitizer.js#L24 The pattern should be `const SAFE_URL_PATTERN = /^(?:(?:https?|mailto|ftp|tel|file|sms|sip):|[^#&/:?]*(?:[#/?]|$))/i` An example of how this is used is like this: `Chat` And when you click the link an application such as Microsoft Teams can open a direct chat to that person. ### Motivation and context I am trying to show a chat link in a popover and this is keeping me from being able to do that while still sanitizing the popover. I'd love to see this make it into 5.3.0. Happy to open a PR if you'd like. Where this change has such low impact I'm hoping it can be added quickly.

### Describe the issue

Using the required attribute in an html input field should cause the browser to scroll up to the field, and display an error message such as 'please fill out this field'.  If the field in question is higher on the page and requires the browser to scroll up, the field is highlighted but the message is not displayed.  It does work correctly if the field that is required is already in the viewport.  This occurs even on bootstraps documentation page. https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/forms/validation/#how-it-works.  Go to the default browser validation section.  Make your browser narrow and scroll the page so the submit button is almost at the top of the viewport.  when you click the submit button, the form scrolls up, but the bubble help message is not displayed.

Scroll so just the submit button is showing and nothing above it.  Submit the form.  The form scrolls up but the bubble help is not displayed.  Then test when the entire form is visible.  It highlights the input field and then correctly displays the bubble help message.  This does not seem like correct behavior.     This works differently on my version of chrome on windows 7.  The bubble help DOES display on that old system but not on my newer windows 10 system (tested both with chrome and edge).

title: Documentation for customizing Bootstrap SCSS leaves out critical information
body:
For 5.3, the page I am referring to is:

https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/customize/sass/

but the issue has been there a long time.

People new to understanding how SCSS -> CSS works, especially in relationship to Bootstrap, need to understand two critically important details that are not currently covered:
* After you have created your `scss/custom.scss` or whatever file, you need to recompile it to create a revised `scss/custom.css` (or whatever) file.
* Subsequent to that recompile, you need to import the *custom* custom.css file, *instead* of the standard Bootstrap one.

Separately, it would be useful to point out that tools like `sass` are available that can watch for changes in your source files, and automatically recompile the CSS when the SCSS file is changed.  Many IDEs also have "file watching" plugins that can do the same thing.

It took me viewing more than a few YouTube videos to figure out why just editing my `custom.scss` file didn't have any effect.

- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

The [Offcanvas](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.2/components/offcanvas/) component has a "fixed" width of 400px (adjustable via `$offcanvas-horizontal-width`) for any breakpoint.

It would be very useful to have different size options similar as Modal also has optional [sizes](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.2/components/modal/#optional-sizes).

Like:

offcanvas-size-sm (default 300px)
offcanvas (default 400px)
offcanvas-size-lg: 500px
offcanvas-size-xl: 600px
offcanvas-size-xxl: 700px

Proposed withs are just an example.



### Motivation and context

This especially useful as screens tend to get wider and wider but one still wants to use a Offcanvas to hide some content by default; like an advanced filtering pane.

title: Image Carousel with Thumbnails
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

A new component that allows users to display a carousel of thumbnailed photos. The component should have the option of customizing the number of images shown on each slide, the size of the thumbnails, and the animation used to change slides.

### Motivation and context

Currently, Bootstrap offers a carousel component that allows users to display a slideshow of images. However, this component does not include thumbnails, which can make it difficult for users to navigate through the images. By adding an Image Carousel with Thumbnails component, users would have an easy way to navigate through a collection of images, such as a product gallery or a portfolio. This would make it more convenient for users to find the specific image they are looking for.
The Image Carousel with Thumbnails component would be useful for showcasing a collection of images, such as a product gallery or a portfolio. The thumbnails allow users to easily navigate to specific images, while the carousel provides a visually engaging way to display the images. This would enhance the user experience by making it easier for users to view the images and find the ones they are interested in.

author: khellendros74

title: Add Horizontal Scroll View for Tabs or  Cards
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

**Overview:**
This proposal aims to introduce a horizontal scroll view feature for any kind of bars and image card components in Bootstrap. This feature will allow users to scroll horizontally within a limited space to view all the items, providing an optimal solution for managing content overflow.

**Goals:** 

1. Improve usability: Enable users to easily navigate through many tabs in a tabnav for example or view multiple image cards without resizing the browser window.
2. Enhance responsiveness: Maintain a consistent and visually pleasing layout across different devices and screen sizes, ensuring that content is accessible without sacrificing the overall design.
3. Preserve Bootstrap's simplicity: Implement a straightforward and intuitive solution that aligns with Bootstrap's philosophy of simplicity and ease of use.





### Motivation and context

I would like to suggest the addition of a horizontal scroll view feature for components such as tabnav as an example or image cards in Bootstrap. This feature would be particularly useful when dealing with a large number of items within a limited horizontal space.

Currently, when there are numerous tabs or multiple image cards displayed in a flex layout, it becomes challenging for users to view all the content without resizing the browser window. By introducing a horizontal scroll view, users will be able to scroll horizontally to see all the items without compromising the layout or requiring window resizing.

This enhancement would greatly improve the usability and accessibility of Bootstrap components, especially in scenarios where a large number of features or images need to be displayed within a confined space.

I believe that implementing a horizontal scroll view aligns with Bootstrap's philosophy of simplicity and responsiveness, as it provides an intuitive solution for managing content overflow horizontally. The feature would enhance the overall user experience and ensure that Bootstrap remains a versatile and adaptable framework for building responsive websites.

d-none d-sm-block mx-auto mb-3

title: CI: set minimal permissions on workflow calibreapp-image-actions.yml
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

Hi!

I'm here to suggest that you set minimal permissions to your workflow [calibreapp-image-actions.yml](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/workflows/calibreapp-image-actions.yml), because currently it doesn't specify the permissions for its jobs and their privileges are being determined by GitHub's defaults. I noticed that all of your other workflows already have the permissions defined, and the change I propose is would be very similar.

IIUC, the job called on [calibreapp-image-actions.yml](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/workflows/calibreapp-image-actions.yml) requires a `pull-requests: write` permission, so I'd give this permission job-level, and grant a read-only permission as top-level.

If you have a reason not to define the permissions on that specific workflow, let me know! Otherwise, if you agree with this change I'm available to contribute with a PR to add them and close this issue.

### Motivation and context

Defining minimal permissions secures you against erroneous or malicious behaviour from external jobs you call from your workflow. It's specially important for the case they get compromised, for example. Adicionally, it's a recommendation from [GitHub itself](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/security-guides/automatic-token-authentication) and also from other security tools, such as [Scorecards](https://github.com/ossf/scorecard) and [StepSecurity](https://github.com/step-security).

#### Additional Context
I'm Diogo and I work on Google's Open Source Security Team([GOSST](https://github.com/diogoteles08#about-gosst-ghost)) in cooperation with the Open Source Security Foundation ([OpenSSF](https://openssf.org/)). My core job is to suggest and implement security changes on widely used open source projects 😊

title: Change the color value of some classes in the card
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

Change the color value of some classes in the card to be written like the link color, that is, the calculation of RGBA+transparency, so as to realize the function of adjusting the transparency of the font in Bootstrap itself, because there is already a transparency class of the font class, such as .text- opacity-25.

However, the current .text-opacity-25 cannot adjust the transparency of .card-text, which can be achieved by changing the writing method of related classes, which does not seem to damage too much.

The known related classes are as follows:

- .card-footer
- .card-header
- .card-title
- .card-link
- .card-body
- .card-text

.card-link currently has no specific color setting, but only involves the spacing of elements. I hope you can add some useful styles or change the name, it feels a bit mismatched between the name and the actual function.



### Motivation and context


Almost all card classes are involved. Card itself is an excellent layout structural component. It should use the newly introduced link-like operation of Bootstrap, and in the current color settings of these classes, custom attributes are undefined Yes, is it an active reserved custom name?


By the way, if it works, will it be added in the next stable version? There may not be enough time.

title: Missing $primary-text-emphasis-dark variable
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

The problem is your update a minor version (5.2.3 -> 5.3.0) and have a very little Breaking Change 

variable `$primary-text-emphasis-dark` not exist on 5.2.3 and is required on 5.3.0

```
SassError: Undefined variable.
   ╷
55 │     "primary": $primary-text-emphasis-dark,
   │                ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
   ╵
  node_modules/bootstrap/scss/_maps.scss 55:16  @import
  src/styles.scss 9:9                           root stylesheet
```

The fix is very easy to implement, just need include the new file `variables-dark` on main styles

```
@import "../node_modules/bootstrap/scss/variables";
@import "../node_modules/bootstrap/scss/variables-dark";
 ...
@import "../node_modules/bootstrap/scss/maps";
```

Some automated process may throw an error, because it's a minor update and it should be backwards compatible.





### Reduced test cases

---

Windows

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Microsoft Edge

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

5.3.0

title: Breadcrumbs: Implement text/links colors as variables
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

Breadcrumbs have the following color variables:
$breadcrumb-bg, $breadcrumb-divider-color, $breadcrumb-active-color

So it's possible to change the background of breadcrumbs but not possible to change color of text/links of breadcrumbs with variables.

Introduction of the following variables will enhance Bootstrap 5:
$breadcrumb-link-color
$breadcrumb-link-decoration
$breadcrumb-link-hover-color
$breadcrumb-link-hover-decoration
$breadcrumb-link-text-color

default values would be the same as links


### Motivation and context

Flexibility and customization of the theme/colors

### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

When modifying a CSS variable in the .table class, all variants are affected.
Example:

`.table {
  --bs-table-striped-bg: lightblue;
}`

Wouldn't it be appropriate for it to only affect the default variant?

Thanks.

### Reduced test cases

https://codepen.io/marcoarib/pen/VwVZPwa

### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Windows

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Chrome

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

5.3.0

title: Documentation of navbar, Color schemes: border-bottom

### Describe the issue

In the [documentation of the navbar, section Color schemes](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/components/navbar/#color-schemes), the first example has two additional classes. They are not used in the other examples nor in the 5.2 docs. The first is `border-bottom`. Why is there a border? It's not explained. And it seems to me that it is not really needed. The second is `border-bottom-dark`. AFAIK this is not a Bootstrap class. I also can't see that it is used for the example.

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

5.3.0

title: Seamless Integration with JavaScript Frameworks
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

Bootstrap is a widely-used front-end framework known for its simplicity and versatility. To stay relevant and address the evolving needs of developers, it is crucial for Bootstrap to provide seamless integration with popular JavaScript frameworks such as React, Angular, and Vue.js. The motivation behind this proposal is to enhance Bootstrap's compatibility and interoperability with these frameworks, enabling developers to build robust and modern web applications with ease.


### Proposal:
Optimized Components: Identify and optimize common components for seamless integration.
Official Framework-specific Libraries: Develop official libraries for easy integration with React, Angular, and Vue.js.
Documentation and Tutorials: Create comprehensive guides for integrating Bootstrap with these frameworks.
Community Engagement: Foster collaboration to address integration issues and share experiences.

### Motivation and context

### Motivation: 
Bootstrap needs to seamlessly integrate with popular JavaScript frameworks like React, Angular, and Vue.js to meet developers' evolving needs.

### Context:
JavaScript frameworks like React, Angular, and Vue.js are widely used for dynamic user interfaces. Bootstrap should optimize components and offer smooth integration to overcome integration challenges
author: ayoubkhan558

title: Increase discoverability of "range" control by using the term "slider"
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

I wanted to add the UI widget commonly called a [slider](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Slider_(computing)) to my web page, but when I searched the Bootstrap documentation for "slider" all I got was results about the carousel component. Searching Google for "Bootstrap slider", the first results were about the carousel component, followed by a link to a third-party slider implementation for old versions of Bootstrap. It took some further searching to discover that Bootstrap 5 does have a built-in slider feature but calls it a "range". You seem to be aware that this type of control is generally called a slider, since there are [three Bootstrap icons of sliders](https://icons.getbootstrap.com/?q=sliders).

It might help the discoverability of the "range" control if it were findable using the term "slider".

### Reduced test cases

N/A

### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

macOS

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Safari

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

5.3.0

title: Custom popover no longer works after upgrading to 5.3.0
body: ### Prerequisites - [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues - [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems - [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Describe the issue I used to pass a function to the `content` option of `Popover` as follows, ```javascript new bootstrap.Popover(popoverTriggerEl, { content: get_popover_content, }); ``` to trigger a popover defined as ```html click me ``` where `this.dataset` contains the `data` of the field ```javascript function get_popover_content() { if (this.dataset.tagInfo) { // } // } ``` This no longer works after upgrading to bootstrap 5.3.0 (`this` points to `document` and `this.dataset` is `null` now) so I am wondering what is the proper way to do it with the new version. Note that the [documentation](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/components/popovers/) says > If a function is given, it will be called with its this reference set to the element that the popover is attached to. and `this` is supposed to be the `` element. ### Reduced test cases [codepen](https://codepen.io/bopeng/pen/XWyWRra) The key to look is `console.log(this)`, which points to the document and not the `` element. If you change the `bootstrap` version to `5.2.3`, `this` points to the button itself. ### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on? macOS ### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on? Chrome ### What version of Bootstrap are you using? v5.3.0

title: v5.3.0 Broken floating label background for disabled Input[type=text] and textarea
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

By default the floating labels of disabled input texts and textareas have a white background (i.e. the body background). 
For select inputs it works as expected. Please see these examples.

![Screenshot 2023-06-08 at 16 31 30](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/assets/220102/2cc2f3e8-412f-4b0a-9aaa-5f3ba239aadb)
![Screenshot 2023-06-08 at 16 29 01](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/assets/220102/ad6c9ec2-42d8-42f4-af22-c8e320fffea5)



### Reduced test cases

Reproducer: https://codepen.io/virtualized/pen/ZEmYLXq

My workaround for now is simply adding and `!important`
```css
.form-floating > :disabled ~ label::after {
  background-color: $input-disabled-bg !important;
}
```

### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

macOS

Safari

v.5.3.0

title: Certain Bootstrap Sass color variables error with "is not a color" if used in a Sass function
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

Calling a Sass color function on a Bootstrap var of which the value is set to a CSS variable will error with an "... is not a color" Sass compilation error.

Given that 5.3.0 has changed the values of a lot of Sass variables to CSS variables, this breaks any code that uses those vars in places where colors are derived (which includes common mixins like `button-variant`). This caused no issues in 5.2.x.

I believe this is the same underlying issue as came up in the discussion on #38687 without a test case, see the one below for a minimal reproduction.

### Reduced test cases

https://stackblitz.com/edit/stackblitz-starters-tetgwb?file=src%2Fstyles.scss

If that doesn't work, it can be boiled down to:
```scss
@import '../node_modules/bootstrap/scss/bootstrap';
div.foo {
    background: darken($card-bg, 20%); // Compile will error here
}
```

### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

macOS

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

_No response_

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

5.3.0

title: Form group and control for select are displayed drastically differently on desktop and mobile
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

In my app, I have three dropdowns. When in desktop screen mode, the options display as expected, large and below the `<select>` dropdown.

However, when I click the mobile emulator in the Dev Tools, the options appear up and to the right of the dropdown, and in a tiny font that is barely readable. 

I have not seen this before and I have not been able to find any other instances of this being a problem for anyone in my research. 

Including link to question on Stack Overflow as well: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/76444592/bootstrap-5-dropdowns-look-atrocious-on-mobile-off-to-the-side-small-etc-n

### Reduced test cases

Unfortunately, I am not able to put together a minimally viable test case for you, because I am using Bootstrap in Vite and with Vue 3 and the codepen doesn't allow for those options. I can share the site it is on, hopefully that will help. I am also including a zip file of the entire project since a reduced test case is not possible. 

https://evilgeniuscreative.com/demo_ncdot/ is the live site

The zip file is here, since it's too large to upload by a little bit: **https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/13HTgdC4kph35oQnQb7DkudPRw1aec9iF?usp=sharing**

This is the screenshot of the site at a desktop width and mode:
![Screenshot 2023-06-09 at 10 46 55 PM](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/assets/4052357/87409a41-dc5f-4739-8b0e-361bba176515)

This is the screenshot of the site at a mobile emulation in Chrome
![Screenshot 2023-06-09 at 10 47 06 PM](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/assets/4052357/c595b7a1-6a49-4bf9-a2c9-5a1bfa2bf59f)

**NOTE**: These screenshots are not showing up in the view that I can see once I submit it, so I included them in the google drive folder link above instead. I do not see any of the attachments actually attached, so they are there.

I'm afraid I have no idea what's going on with this to give you even a starting point. Sorry. 

Thank you.

macOS

title: Examples download "bootstrap-5.3.0-examples.zip" is missing file for dark mode "color-modes.js"
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

The Examples downloadable zip file found at
https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/releases/download/v5.3.0/bootstrap-5.3.0-examples.zip
is missing JavaScript file "color-modes.js", which is referenced by the starter template (bootstrap-5.3.0-examples.zip\bootstrap-5.3.0-examples\starter-template).  It is used for Dark/Light mode toggle.

A work-around is to copy the file from the Demo starter template at https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/assets/js/color-modes.js

Would be a nice idea for convenience to include it in the download so that the Dark/Light mode examples work out of the box.



### Motivation and context

It would allow the downloadable examples to work out of the box without any additional work.
author: mark2016

title: `btn-subtle` button class
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

I love the 5.3 dark mode - it looks awesome! One thing that has caught me out though is that `btn-light` is a "light" button, where in light mode it is a "subtle" button.

I'd like to propose a new button modifier "btn-subtle" which will respect the theme, tinting the button as suitable for either dark or light mode.

In effect:

* When in light mode `btn-subtle` would be the same as `btn-light`, while
* In dark mode `btn-subtle` would be the same as `btn-dark`


### Motivation and context

I've been using `btn-light` in a number of places to act as a subtle tint. That worked prior to dark mode, but breaks now.

Another option for the name would be `btn-tertiary` perhaps.

title: Responsive Collapse
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

It would be very helpful to have responsive classes for the collapse component.  For example adding a .collapse-sm class would mean that the content is hidden by default only if the viewport is smaller than 576px.

Example:
![output](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/assets/120429276/ab18916f-727c-4086-8b61-d63cb08856f6)

Example Code: https://jsfiddle.net/mvLxytk7/1/



### Motivation and context

It's already possible to achieve this by using the .navbar-collapse, and .navbar-expand{-sm|-md|-lg|-xl|-xxl} classes like in [this example](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/components/navbar/#responsive-behaviors). But I think it would be easier and make more sense if one would be able to accomplish this without having to use a navbar.

body:

Previously, it was possible to color table rows (and perhaps cells, which I've not used) in a different color using `text-bg-*`, for example ``. I have primarily preferred them over the `table-*` classes because of the more uniform look with other elements and the stronger, more distinct colors. The documentation does not explicitly state that the `text-bg-*` or `bg-*` classes cannot be used on table elements, nor do the table docs mention that you have to use the `table-*` classes. When trying to do this now, the row remains default colored, because the rule for `.table > :not(caption) > * > * `, which sets ` background-color: var(--bs-table-bg);`, takes precedent on the ``s. ### Reduced test cases ```html

title: Tabs not working with Shadow Root
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

Tabs do not work inside a shadow root. The cause of this is the following function  in `SelectorEngine`:

```
getElementFromSelector(element) {
      const selector = getSelector(element);
      return selector ? SelectorEngine.findOne(selector) : null;
    },
```

instead of calling `findOne` like that, it should be called using `SelectorEngine.findOne(selector, element.getRootNode())` which would solve the problem.

This solution assumes that the tabs and containers lie within the same DOM, but this is a fair assumption.

### Reduced test cases

JSfiddle was simpler for me. I just put 

https://jsfiddle.net/djae3guf/

title: $form-feedback-valid-color and $form-feedback-invalid-color do not apply to box-shadow
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

I'm trying to change the highlight colour on valid inputs without changing the colour of 'success'.

I want to keep 'success' as green in a small number of locations for buttons, alerts, etc, but on a form with lots of inputs having so much green is just too much and doesn't look good.

I noticed there is a variable for this, so in my Sass file I tried:
`$form-feedback-valid-color: $secondary;`
and this works to change the border colour, but the box-shadow colour is still green. Looking at the source files I can see that the box-shadow is indeed hard-coded to use 'success' for valid, and 'danger' for invalid.

I'm able to get the result I want by overriding all of $form-validation-states, but think that overriding $form-feedback-valid-color by itself should be enough.

How it looks:
![image](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/assets/42629973/5fcebc98-d053-4dc7-b7d6-e96bbbe8668c)

What I expect:
![image](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/assets/42629973/77bcbd38-291b-4b81-ad97-faa82663f650)


### Reduced test cases

// 1. Include functions first (so you can manipulate colors, SVGs, calc, etc)
@import "../node_modules/bootstrap/scss/functions";

// 2. Include any default variable overrides here

$secondary: #004a98;
$form-feedback-valid-color: $secondary;
```

title: Modal hide listener to modal-backdrop
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

Add `modal-backdrop` to the hide modal listener



### Motivation and context

The modal-dialog is positioned using the `modal` container (position fixed and full screen).

You can specify position: absolute for `modal`, position: relative for `parent element` if needed, and this allows for custom `modal-dialog` positioning.

But this does not hide modal by clicking outside the `modal-dialog` area. This is because only `modal` is added to the hide modal listener.

![custom modal position](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/assets/129936178/44877d13-913b-4bbc-b47f-4b89caff9dcc)
![modal show](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/assets/129936178/21085653-23da-459d-b610-267f9b98ce9c)
