title: Element.setCapture() is deprecated. Use Element.setPointerCapture() instead
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

Hello,

I got a warning in the developers tool in Firefox in mobile view simulation, I thought that it is in my code, I tried the official bootstrap website, I got he same warning.

`Element.setCapture() is deprecated. Use Element.setPointerCapture() instead. For more help https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Element/setPointerCapture`


![setcapture_firefox](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/assets/13575753/bffe7322-a0fb-4bc9-9be6-df11045240e6)


### Reduced test cases

[](https://getbootstrap.com/)

Click on the the dots.


Chat GPT
------------------------
If you're seeing the warning "Element.setCapture() is deprecated. Use Element.setPointerCapture() instead" in Firefox when using Bootstrap, you can solve it by updating your version of Bootstrap to a newer version that uses `setPointerCapture()` instead of `setCapture()`.

Alternatively, you can add the following code to your JavaScript file to replace all instances of `setCapture()` with `setPointerCapture()`:

```
if (typeof Element.prototype.setPointerCapture === 'undefined') {
  Element.prototype.setPointerCapture = Element.prototype.setCapture;
}

```
This code checks if `setPointerCapture()` is supported by the browser, and if not, it creates an alias to `setCapture()`. This way, any code that uses `setCapture()` will still work without generating the deprecation warning.

You can add this code to your JavaScript file before any other code that uses `setCapture()`, or you can add it to a separate file and include it in your HTML file before your other JavaScript files.
-----------

That didn't solve the warning :(

----------------

If updating to a newer version of Bootstrap or adding the code to replace `setCapture()` with `setPointerCapture()` did not solve the problem, you can try the following:

1. Check if any other JavaScript libraries or plugins you are using are also using `setCapture()`. If so, update those libraries or plugins to a version that uses `setPointerCapture()` instead.

2. If you are using a custom script that uses `setCapture()`, you can update it to use `setPointerCapture()` instead. Here's an example of how to update the code:

   Before:
   
```
   element.setCapture();
   document.addEventListener('mousemove', handleMouseMove);
   document.addEventListener('mouseup', handleMouseUp);

```

   After:
   
```
   element.setPointerCapture(pointerId);
   document.addEventListener('pointermove', handleMouseMove);
   document.addEventListener('pointerup', handleMouseUp);
```

   In this example, `pointerId` is the ID of the pointer that you want to capture. You can use `null` to capture all pointers.

3. If you are unable to update the code to use `setPointerCapture()`, you can disable the deprecation warning in Firefox. To do this, type `about:config` in the Firefox address bar and press Enter. Search for the preference `dom.events.testing.asyncClipboard.setCapture.warning` and set it to `false`. This will disable the warning for all websites.

Note that disabling the warning is not recommended, as it may hide other important warnings related to deprecated features. It's always best to update your code to use the latest, non-deprecated features.
------------------------

Is there will be an update to bootstrap to fix that waning? 
Does that mean some bootstrap function using touch on mobile will stop working using current bootstrap version 5.3?

Regards,

Windows

Firefox

5.3.0

title: Allow dropdowns to have a configurable container
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

As mentioned in the [docs for popovers](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/components/popovers/#custom-container)/tooltips, the `container` option is useful for avoiding rendering problems in more complex situations. Since dropdowns are also popper based, it'd be useful for it to support the same option.

### Motivation and context

In my particular case, I'd like to have a dropdown within a `overflow:auto` parent, but allow the `.dropdown-menu` to not be clipped by the size of that parent.

The default behavior of email validation is just checking `any number of characters` then an `@` symbol and `any character` that's it.
I want to check for a correct validation so I tried to put a `pattern` tag in email input with following pattern.
`pattern="[a-z0-9._%+-]+@[a-z0-9.-]+\.[a-z]{2,}$"` But the pattern is working (still default behavior) and Browser throwing error as follow:
`Pattern attribute value [a-z0-9._%+-]+@[a-z0-9.-]+\.[a-z]{2,}$ is not a valid regular expression: Uncaught SyntaxError: Invalid regular expression: /[a-z0-9._%+-]+@[a-z0-9.-]+\.[a-z]{2,}$/v: Invalid character in character class`

I tried to remove `novalidate` from `form` tag to bypass the Bootstrap's Javascript. But same error.
As soon as I hit the `@` key browser throwing same error.

Could anyone check where I am wrong?

Suggestion: Please upgrade `https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/forms/validation/` page to provide in depth detail for form validator. I also wanted to use my custom javascript code along with Bootstrap's form validator but It is not working.

title: 'this' ref not set in function passed as title option in tooltip config
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

In the docs, it says 'If a function is given, it will be called with its this reference set to the element that the popover is attached to.' But this no longer seems to be true in 5.3.

Now, I needed to pass an argument to the function, and that argument is a reference to the element that the popover/tooltip is attached to. 

I looked through the internal functions '_resolvePossibleFunction' and 'execute' and this is how it appears to be structured now.

So the docs should be updated to reflect this.

title: Missing `--bs-card-color` CSS variable in Bootstrap v5.3.0
body: ### Prerequisites - [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues - [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems - [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Describe the issue Upon upgrading to Bootstrap v5.3.0 from v5.2.0, the CSS variable `--bs-card-color` appears to be no longer defined. This variable was being utilized to style certain components within the project, and its absence is now resulting in visual discrepancies. The documentation and changes in the new version do not seem to indicate any details about the removal or possible replacement of this variable. The disappearance of this variable has specifically affected the day and night themes on the website - the text is consistently black regardless of the theme. Here is the error message that occurs: `Error: --bs-card-color is not defined` Could clarification be provided as to why this variable was removed or if it was replaced with another variable? If this is an oversight, it would be appreciated if it could be addressed in the upcoming release. ### Reduced test cases The problem manifests when toggling the website theme from day to night. Specifically, the text within the Card component retains its black color instead of changing to an appropriate color for the night theme, thus making it practically invisible against the dark background. ### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on? macOS ### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on? Chrome ### What version of Bootstrap are you using? ^5.2.3

title: Please log the affected DOM node to the console for error "Bootstrap doesn't allow more than one instance per element. Bound instance: bs.tooltip."
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

The error message "Bootstrap doesn't allow more than one instance per element. Bound instance: bs.tooltip." is pretty unhelpful for debugging. Especially when I have 100+ of these errors on CI after upgrading to Bootstrap 5. It would be very helpful to get a `console.log` of the affected DOM node.

General question about this topic unrelated to the feature request: if I understand the following [discussion](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues/33940) correctly, tooltip + dropdown on a single element are unsupported now and the hacky workaround is to nest a `span` with the tooltip inside of the dropdown menu. Is that the recommended method? Does this even help users with screenreaders? Also, I think this messes up the positioning of the Tooltip. Are there better ways to achieve this?



### Motivation and context

Easier debugging while doing a Bootstrap upgrade.

### Describe the issue

This is a minor issue with the fade animation and it appears to be a new issue with the latest version 5.3. When toggling tabs with the fade effect, the tab content no longer fades out before the new content fades in. The content just disappears before the fade in. title: Offcanvas multiple same events
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

Using JS to create bootstrap.Offcanvas, a 'keydown' event listener is added each time the Offcanvas is opened (and closed by button data-bs-toggle).
As the Offcanvas object seems to use an anonymous function with addEventListener, it is not possible to remove the listener.

The only solution found is to add a listener to 'hidden.bs.offcanvas' and to call dispose() on the object.

### Reduced test cases

The only solution found is to add a listener to 'hidden.bs.offcanvas' and to call dispose() on the object.

title: Invisible focus outline in chrome for button element
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

The <button> element is typically expected to display an outline when it receives focus. However, in some cases, the default styling of buttons, particularly in Bootstrap, hides the outline for buttons. As a result, the outline is not visible when a button receives focus, and this behavior is observed in certain browsers like Chrome.

While it may be a browser-specific issue, there are ways to address this problem within the context of Bootstrap. 

It's because In `_reboot.scss` we set:
```
button {
  border-radius: 0;
}
```

title: Responsive offcanvas placement
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

Allow one offcanvas component to have different placements based on the screen dimensions.

### Solution

Introduce a set of offcanvas-placement classes including breakpoints:
`.offcanvas-{breakpoint}-{position}`, so that one offcanvas would have a different placement based on the current viewport size.

### Demo

For example, a `<div class="offcanvas offcanvas-bottom offcanvas-md-end">` would be displayed at the bottom on small devices, and to the right as soon as the viewport's size would be medium.

Solution in a smartphone screen:

![offcanvas-bottom-md-right](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/assets/925443/48580f2d-e688-48b0-be19-3cc9e75db10c)

Same offcanvas in a laptop screen:

![offcanvas-bottom-md-right-md](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/assets/925443/e5973072-8e44-4287-96ee-40a6bcb83c42)


### Motivation and context

In current Bootstrap 5.3, the available placements of offcanvas are sub-optimal in some screen sizes, in the sense that:

* `.offcanvas-end` covers completely the underneath content in a mobile screen layout, whereas `.offcanvas-bottom` would leave part of it visible.
* `.offcanvas-bottom` cuts too much vertical content in a laptop's landscape layout - compared to what would be left visible with `.offcanvas-end`.

Current `.offcanvas-end` on a smartphone screen:

![offcanvas-right](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/assets/925443/a85ae96e-f6ec-4d7d-b5d6-017ebd8c66c6)

Current `.offcanvas-bottom` on a mid-size laptop screen:

![offcanvas-bottom](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/assets/925443/b9872bc8-8815-47cc-b350-f2661e0112cd)

title: TypeError when Creating new bootstrap Object Before Adding to DOM - 5.3.0
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

After upgrading directly from Bootstrap 5.0.x to 5.3.0 in a project recently, I noticed a new exception being thrown from some of my javascript classes:
`TypeError: 'querySelector' called on an object that does not implement interface Element.`

After debugging and finding the offending line, it landed on the instantiation of a new bootstrap component object - either with `new bootstrap[Component]` or with `bootstrap[Component].getOrCreateInstance`.

In version 5.0.*, it worked fine when creating a new HTMLElement with `document.createElement`, then using that element in a Bootstrap component before it had been attached to the DOM. Now it throws the aforementioned error if it hasn’t been added to the DOM first. 

### Reduced test cases

I did my best to look through the documentation for a reference to this behavior, and couldn’t find it. I apologize if it is there somewhere. If it isn’t _and_ this is intended functionality, it is probably best to add this tidbit to the documentation to prevent similar hard-to-diagnose errors in the future. It might also be a good idea to throw a more concise exception. Thanks!

### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

macOS

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Firefox

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

5.0.1, 5.3.0

title: Files not been copied to project source folder after added with nuget package manager in Visual Studio 2022 - v.17.6.5
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

I created a new `.net 6.0 MVC Web` project and removed the preinstalled bootstrap from `wwwroot/lib` because I wanted to add it with NuGet Package Manager. After installing the package (bootstrap v5.3.0), files are shown as a reference in my `wwwroot`, and when I try to link them from code browser gives not found error cause they don't actually exists in my project.

Now I'm trying this: `<link rel="stylesheet" href="~/css/bootstrap.min.css" />` but as I said is not working. The only solution I've got is to copy those file from `C\..nuget\packages` to my `wwwroot` but I've read that this reference is actually an intended behaviour from Microsoft, so it should be a way to use those referenced files but I can't seem to find it.

Also [this](https://developercommunity.visualstudio.com/t/NuGet-Package-Content-is-not-copied/25259#T-N244926) comment suggests that is a nuget bug not a Visual Studio bug.

I have a post [here ](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/76673639/visual-studio-2022-v-17-6-5-link-installed-nuget-package-css-file-in-layout?noredirect=1#comment135244895_76673639) with a screen 

### Reduced test cases

Steps to reproduce:

1. Start new .net core 6 MVC project
2. Remove preinstalled bootstrap files 
3. Add with Nuget Package Manager latest stable version of bootstrap (v.5.3.0 in my case)

As a result, files are showed in `wwwroot/css` and `wwwroot/js` folder but as a simple reference, they are not physically there - so when I try to build the project, not styles or scripts of bootstrap are loaded because they could not be found.

Windows

Chrome

v5.3.0

title: Using `hidden` attribute with `d-flex`
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

The display property of `.d-flex` overrides the display property of `[hidden]`.

E.g. this div should be hidden, but is not: `<div class="d-flex" hidden>`

I can use the the `d-none` class, and it overrides `d-flex` because it's defined later, but IMO, `[hidden]` should also override `d-flex`.

Essentially, the `hidden` attribute works for all elements except those with `d-*, so it's more of a consistent results issue than anything else.

title: Improve button press feedback
body: ### Improvement Bootstrap looks really interessting and nice. But the button press feedback feels boring. Did I pressed the button? I'm not sure. The click behavior feels like disabled. Like pressing a stone. It can be optional. But it would be great if the button feedback got better. Suggestions: **A** **B** **C** (Inspired by Material Design) At best, the user should see that the button has been pressed when lift their finger from the touchscreen. Event if the button is pressed again. For example with the wave effect, which disappears after a certain time. ### Workaround Quick and dirty... to simulate a RL button clicked you can use the following. But it's not optimal. I would delay the release state. And btw. this behavior is maybe not the best, if you want to embed your button. It works better for floating buttons. I would prefer the ripple effect. ```css button.btn:active { transform: translateY(1px); } ``` There are only CSS (pure) implementations of the ripple effect. But with some quirks or limitations. For example with overflow hidden you cannot use badges on edge anymore. Maybe with a wrapper...

title: data-bs-theme="dark" behaves differently compared to CSS class `navbar-dark`
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

When we want to have a navbar using `bg-primary` and need to make text inside of navbar white (bg-body), we could use:
```html
<nav class="navbar bg-primary navbar-dark" aria-label="Top Navigation Bar">
...
</nav>
````
In that case, dropdowns have white background with default text color, like the page itself.
![image](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/assets/4405210/3086dcec-f158-4986-ae8a-bc01036f7540)


With the new recommendation, starting with version 5.3, we should replace `navbar-dark` with `data-bs-theme="dark"`:
```html
<nav class="navbar bg-primary" data-bs-theme="dark" aria-label="Top Navigation Bar">
...
</nav>
````
While navbar itself looks identical, this turns dropdowns and input boxes inside of dropdown in the "dark" mode (black background and light text, while everything else on the page is still white background and black text).
![image](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/assets/4405210/2f6dcb68-9c64-4e83-9c82-029e12cda510)

The problem is that currently there is no way to make dropdown use default background and text colors, while navbar is "dark".

Culprit for this behavior is this rule:
```css
[data-bs-theme=dark] {
    //....
}
```




### Reduced test cases

Codepen for `navbar-dark`: https://codepen.io/nenadvicentic/pen/LYXgvVX
Codepen for (broken) `data-bs-theme="dark"`: https://codepen.io/nenadvicentic/pen/XWyxoaB

### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Windows

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Chrome, Firefox

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

5.3.0

title: Help with Vue 3 adaptation of Carousel
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

Normally I don't need help with the Vue adapation. Usually things are straightforward. However, some sore spots are transitions. They can be difficult, especially because not all transitions are built the same. 

One thing that's presenting an issue to me is the carousel. Previously I somehow made it work by reversing the direction of the start/end carousel items https://github.com/bootstrap-vue-next/bootstrap-vue-next/blob/main/packages/bootstrap-vue-next/src/components/BCarousel/BCarousel.vue#L160 and the carousel item being declared in the transition class -- rather than just being a static class. 

I tried to fix it in this in this diff https://github.com/bootstrap-vue-next/bootstrap-vue-next/compare/main...VividLemon:bootstrap-vue-next:carousel-hack#diff-add493a7ac584c35f6cbfa84f4f62bec7152e3b9c0ff8cd1eed7398d4871b2a1
The previous worked "fine", but it suffered from the mentioned above issues.
I mention it in the code https://github.com/bootstrap-vue-next/bootstrap-vue-next/blob/77fe9cd85cc911bca5aaff41294afead30e52dc4/packages/bootstrap-vue-next/src/components/BCarousel/BCarousel.vue#L153

I can upload a video of what occurs, but the most straightforward way to play with it yourself is to 

```
clone https://github.com/VividLemon/bootstrap-vue-next.git 
git switch carousel-hack
pnpm i
pnpm dev --filter bootstrap-vue-next
```
Then open the dev link that appears
When clicking through the controls, it does two things, first, it stutters around, the second issue, it clips on the ends. It seems as if it's out of sync. I have a hard time believing it's strictly a Vue issue, and more it's an issue with both Vue and Bootstrap. 

It would be a great help to get some official Bootstrap support on this issue, as I seemingly can't get this one done alone.

### Reduced test cases

Linux

Firefox

5.3

### Describe the issue

The [range form element track](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/forms/range/#overview) is practically invisible on most of the screens I have tested. It uses the tertiary background colour which on a white background is very difficult to see.

$form-range-track-bg: var(--#{$prefix}tertiary-bg);

[Contrast check](https://snook.ca/technical/colour_contrast/colour.html#fg=F8F9FA,bg=FFFFFF)

title: Popovers: triggers click and focus can't be combined
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

The documentation says : "How popover is triggered - click | hover | focus | manual. You may pass multiple triggers; separate them with a space. manual cannot be combined with any other trigger."

But if you set **data-bs-trigger="click focus"** the click trigger doesn't work, only the focus trigger works.
I mean if you click again the button to close the popover, it doesn't close.

Bootstrap 5.3.0

title: Replace deprecated`@import`
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

I recommend replacing the use of `@import`, which is deprecated and will be phased out in a few years, with `@use` or something of the like.

### Motivation and context

Since `@import` will be removed eventually, and the feature comes with some [issues](https://sass-lang.com/documentation/at-rules/import/), it would make sense to migrate before the breaking change takes place.

### Describe the issue

While using the latest bootstrap with dart-sass and including bootstrap via scss, the latest dart-sass is warning about a deprecation:
```
DEPRECATION WARNING: Passing percentage units to the global abs() function is deprecated.
In the future, this will emit a CSS abs() function to be resolved by the browser.
To preserve current behavior: math.abs(100%)
To emit a CSS abs() now: abs(#{100%})
More info: https://sass-lang.com/d/abs-percent
```
the stacktrace they show is:
```
node_modules\bootstrap\scss\mixins\_grid.scss 59:12        row-cols()
node_modules\bootstrap\scss\mixins\_grid.scss 85:13        @content
node_modules\bootstrap\scss\mixins\_breakpoints.scss 68:5  media-breakpoint-up()
node_modules\bootstrap\scss\mixins\_grid.scss 72:5         make-grid-columns()
node_modules\bootstrap\scss\_grid.scss 38:3                @import
node_modules\bootstrap\scss\bootstrap.scss 20:9            @import
```
I hope this helps. Tks for your work.

@import '../../node_modules/bootstrap/scss/functions';
@import '../../node_modules/bootstrap/scss/_variables';
@import '../../node_modules/bootstrap/scss/bootstrap';

### Describe the issue

Since both classes should be applied to list group link or button, the `list-group-item` colors overrides `list-group-item-action` colors because `list-group-item` declaration comes after `list-group-item-action` in `list-group.scss` file.

There are separate variables for defining colors independently for `list-group-item` and `list-group-item-action` but they don't really work due to the above issue.

title: Web components: `rem` inside variable declaration
body: ### Prerequisites - [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues - [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems - [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Describe the issue I created some web components using bootstrap as a base. However, after implementing them within our site I notice that the components looks smaller than they should. It looks like this is cause by the fact that some of the bootstrap variables use `rem` to calculate sizes for `sm`, `md` and `lg`. It appears that components within the shadow dom can't use the overall sizing from the root html. ### Reduced test cases To fix this issue, I converted the variables from `rem` to `em` so the can pick the local shadow dom defaults. This seems to have fixed the issue. I'm not sure if this is something that should be change for all bootstrap users. But I figured I'll add it here in case others encounter this issue. ### Original ### After ### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on? Windows, macOS, Linux ### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on? Chrome, Safari ### What version of Bootstrap are you using? 5.3.0-alpha3

title: Isolate of bg color values for components from `body-bg`
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

Many components have a their background color defined by `body-bg`

This means that if a developer wants to change the body background color to, let's say, red, they'd also have to customize all the individual components or hardcode the `body` background color.

My suggestion is to create a new `component-bg` isolated from `body-bg` and use `body-bg` just to define the body.

This `component-bg` would have a value of `body-bg` as a default to minimize breaking changes.


### Motivation and context

As I was creating a new project, I defined a new bg color on `body-bg` and I had to then redefine the color of all the components I was using.

title: Outline buttons have the same active and hover color
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

Outline buttons have the same active and hover color, making them barely usable on mobile where hover stays after the tap, making an impression that active state didn't change.

Sort of workaround applying 15% shading to the button:

```scss
.btn-outline-calculator {
  @include button-outline-variant($gray-100);
}
.btn-outline-calculator:hover {
  background-color: shade-color($gray-100, 15%);
}
```

But I think such shading should be enabled by default?

Another solution might be to use JS to remove hover from the button after some timeout on mobile devices, but I don't exactly know how to do it.

### Reduced test cases

With dark themed HTML:

```html
<button aria-pressed="true" class="btn btn-outline-light active" data-bs-toggle="button">Text</button>
```

Testing on iOS, pressing the button doesn't change the color at all which is very confusing.

iOS

Chrome

5.3.1

### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

Take the [Events documentation in for Collapse](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/components/collapse/#events) as an instance. It shows how to bind to a JavaScript event like this:

>```javascript
> const myCollapsible = document.getElementById('myCollapsible')
> myCollapsible.addEventListener('hidden.bs.collapse', event => {
>   // do something...
> })
> ```

The docs don’t identify the element on which the event will be emitted – is it the trigger or the element that’s hidden?

I would suggest using the same markup throughout a page. That is, use the same identifiers in the “Events” and “Methods” sections that are used in the HTML snippet from the “Example” section.

Alternatively, you might use an ID that makes the referenced element clear, such as `#my-collapse-trigger`, or you simply state that events are emitted from and methods must be called on the trigger element. The “[Navs and tabs](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/components/navs-tabs/#via-javascript)” page does this well, as shown in this example:

> ```javascript
> const triggerTabList = document.querySelectorAll('#myTab button')
> triggerTabList.forEach(triggerEl => {
>   const tabTrigger = new bootstrap.Tab(triggerEl)
> // <more>
> ```

The Modal page is better in this regard. It at least contains the HTML that would make the examples in the “Methods” and “Events” sections work, even though it’s (IMHO) sneakily provided under “Usage > Via data attributes > Toggle”.

### Reduced test cases

(This template seems unsuitable for documentation bugs.)

### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Windows, macOS, Android, iOS, Linux

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Microsoft Edge, Opera

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

v5.3.0

});
```

title: d-grid with checkbox or radio buttons causes scrolling in Chrome on button click
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

When using [checkbox and/or radio buttons](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/forms/checks-radios/#toggle-buttons) inside of a div with [`d-grid`](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/components/buttons/#block-buttons) class button clicks cause browser window to scroll up.

Reproducible on desktop in Chrome, but not in Safari and not in iOS Chrome.

I think the reason is because clicking label focuses on the related input, but all inputs are inside of the parent div 0,0 position and not near the label:

```css
.btn-check {
    position: absolute;
}
```

My current workaround:

```css
.d-grid {
  .btn-check {
    display: none;
  }
}
```

### Reduced test cases

https://codepen.io/iXeron/pen/ExGjvvv

1. Change browser height so not all buttons fit the viewport
2. Scroll to the bottom and click any button close to the bottom
3. Page will scroll up

### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

macOS

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Chrome

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

5.3.1

### Describe the issue

I am using Offcanvas without the data attributes. Instead, I use my own javascript for instantiating the Offcanvas object and toggling it's state.

My offcanvas element contains a `show` class so it is visible by default.
The issue is that a new offcanvas object using this element is considered "hidden" until I call `hide()` and then `show()` (or `toggle()` twice).

To me it seems `new bootstrap.Offcanvas(myElement)` does not check if there is a `show` class and sets the state properly.

title: getbootstrap.com/docs/ - Documentation for 'Modal' Dialog 'Via JavaScript' section lacks viable examples
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

Note: This is about documentation, rather than bootstrap core. I cannot find a repo for getbootstrap.com directly. 

The 'usage' section of Modal documentation  ( https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/components/modal/#usage ) discusses how to toggle and dismiss modals, the examples result in an action occurring (e.g., the modal is shown, toggled etc)

The following section, 'Via JavaScript' ( https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/components/modal/#via-javascript ) discusses how to obtain a Modal object reference but contains no actionable samples - it took me a while to realize that the subsequent 'methods' section exposed how to actually show a dialog.

I think that replacing the code block within 'Via Javascript' from

```
const myModal = new bootstrap.Modal(document.getElementById('myModal'), options)
// or
const myModalAlternative = new bootstrap.Modal('#myModal', options)
```

To:

```
const myModal = new bootstrap.Modal(document.getElementById('myModal'), options)
// or
const myModalAlternative = new bootstrap.Modal('#myModal', options)
// Show a dialog, see 'Methods' for more JavaScript functionality
myModal.show();
```

Would make a lot of sense from a reader point of view, particularly as the 'Options' and 'Methods' sections are at the same h3 heading depth as the javascript section (I can understand why, but I assumed they were unrelated documentation to what I was after). 

### Reduced test cases

As above, documentation change only. 

### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Linux

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

_No response_

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

5.3

title: .list-group-item-action .list-group-item-link
body:
### Prerequisites - [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests - [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Proposal I am requesting, please, this syntax: ```html
A second link item A third link item A fourth link item
``` And it would render identically to how this looks: ```html
  • A first link item
  • A second link item
  • A third link item
``` The only difference is it would have the large `.list-group-item-action` hit targets that we love. i.e. there are no dead pixels around the link in the items. ### Motivation and context In many contexts of using list groups, it can be necessary to show a blue underline, otherwise people won't know it is clickable. But using the `ul.list-group > li.list-group-item > a` syntax makes small hit targets, which are suboptimal.
author: fulldecent

title: Consistent naming conventions for theme-color unmapping
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

This should have no functional consequences but would perhaps improve the quality and readability.

It appears there are two approaches and I think the later below is clearer:

```scss
@each $color, $value in $theme-colors {
  .btn-#{$color} {
  ....
```

vs

```scss
@each $state in map-keys($theme-colors) {
  .list-group-item-#{$state} {
...
```

Put differently, I feel `$state` better expresses that these are in fact color mode states e.g. "dark" state or "light" state or "my-custom-mode" state; NOT a color per se.

I know this is a nitpick but definitely felt my cognitive effort wasted attempting to figure this out the first perusal of the code. Happy to help if folks agree here. Feel free to close if it's a stupid idea ;)



### Motivation and context

- Less wasted cognitive effort to read the code
- Easier onboarding understanding for folks new to the codebase

title: Deprecation Passing percentage units to the global abs()
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

<w> Deprecation Passing percentage units to the global abs() function is deprecated.
<w> In the future, this will emit a CSS abs() function to be resolved by the browser.
<w> To preserve current behavior: math.abs(100%)
<w> To emit a CSS abs() now: abs(#{100%})
<w> More info: https://sass-lang.com/d/abs-percent

### Reduced test cases

<w> node_modules\bootstrap\scss\vendor\_rfs.scss 57:14         divide()
<w> node_modules\bootstrap\scss\mixins\_grid.scss 59:12        row-cols()
<w> node_modules\bootstrap\scss\mixins\_grid.scss 85:13        @content
<w> node_modules\bootstrap\scss\mixins\_breakpoints.scss 68:5  media-breakpoint-up()
<w> node_modules\bootstrap\scss\mixins\_grid.scss 72:5         make-grid-columns()

title: React Build Fails Using Bootstrap SCSS v5.3.2
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

It should be noted that this issue wasn't encountered when running in localhost, only when I attempted to make a product build. By running `npm run build-dev` (our build script for inserting dev environment variables, same issue replicated with prod), the following error was encountered:

```
alexfromsocal@Stevens-MacBook-Pro % npm run build-dev

> <project name and location>
> firebase use dev && env-cmd -f .env.development.json npm run build

Now using alias dev (<project environment>)

> <project name and location>
> react-scripts build

Creating an optimized production build...
Failed to compile.

./src/assets/scss/theme.scss
ParserError: Syntax Error at line: 1, column 29


npm ERR! code ELIFECYCLE
npm ERR! errno 1
npm ERR! <project name> build: `react-scripts build`
npm ERR! Exit status 1
npm ERR! 
npm ERR! Failed at the<project name> build script.
npm ERR! This is probably not a problem with npm. There is likely additional logging output above.

npm ERR! A complete log of this run can be found in:
npm ERR!     /Users/alexfromsocal/.npm/_logs/2023-09-15T00_23_25_256Z-debug.log
npm ERR! code ELIFECYCLE
npm ERR! errno 1
npm ERR! <project name> build-dev: `firebase use dev && env-cmd -f .env.development.json npm run build`
npm ERR! Exit status 1
npm ERR! 
npm ERR! Failed at the <project name> build-dev script.
npm ERR! This is probably not a problem with npm. There is likely additional logging output above.

npm ERR! A complete log of this run can be found in:
npm ERR!     /Users/alexfromsocal/.npm/_logs/2023-09-15T00_23_25_299Z-debug.log`
```

Based on the given issue and what I had read on some other Github Issues and Stackover Flow posts, it may have regarded SCSS syntax of `#{$my-variable-name}` wrapping, as opposed to `$my-variable-name`.

### Reduced test cases

I combed through the initial theme.scss file, commenting out every import. As I uncommented each import, I further reduced it down to this import: `@import "~bootstrap/scss/bootstrap";`. By not including bootstrap, my builds would complete.

In this node module file `bootstrap/scss/bootstrap`, I further narrowed it down to to the `_pagination.scss` file. Again, commented out the file and the program built. 

FIX:
I found that by changing line 75 `margin-left: $pagination-margin-start;` to `margin-left: #{$pagination-margin-start};`, react-scripts built the app successfully. 

SUGGESTED FIX:
Wrap the variable so that _pagination.scss line 75 is now `margin-left: #{$pagination-margin-start};`

### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

macOS

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Chrome, Firefox

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

v5.3.2

body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues

### Describe the issue

After updating to Bootstrap 5.3.2 we have errors where modals are failing to open due to the query selector failing as invalid 

```
selector-engine.js:41 Uncaught DOMException: Failed to execute 'querySelector' on 'Element': '#j_id22:exampleModal' is not a valid selector.
    at Object.findOne (https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/bootstrap@latest/dist/js/bootstrap.bundle.min.js:6:8558)
    at Object.getElementFromSelector (https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/bootstrap@latest/dist/js/bootstrap.bundle.min.js:6:9299)
    at HTMLButtonElement.<anonymous> (https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/bootstrap@latest/dist/js/bootstrap.bundle.min.js:6:55221)
    at HTMLDocument.n (https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/bootstrap@latest/dist/js/bootstrap.bundle.min.js:6:4238)
```

The target is like `data-bs-target="#j_id22:exampleModal"`. This is a common auto generated identifier from JSF where `:` is used as a seperator.

This code works in 5.3.1 but fails in 5.3.2

title: <dialog>: The Dialog element
body:
### Prerequisites - [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests - [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Proposal Implement native `` element in Bootstrap for enhanced accessibility and usability. ## Proposal to Implement Native `` Element in Bootstrap [MDN - The dialog element](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/dialog) ## Introduction The native HTML `` element has recently gained full browser support. It offers built-in accessibility and usability features, making it an ideal choice for modal dialogs. This proposal suggests that Bootstrap should adopt the `` element to enhance its dialog components. ## Benefits - **Accessibility**: Implicit `aria-modal="true"` for modal dialogs. - **Usability**: Built-in `.showModal()` and `.show()` methods for rendering. - **Standardization**: Native HTML element, no need for custom implementations. ## Example Code ```html

Dialog content here

 ``` ## Built-in Methods for `` ### `.show()` - Opens the dialog as a non-modal. ```javascript const dialog = document.getElementById("myDialog"); dialog.show(); ``` ### `.showModal()` - Opens the dialog as a modal, making all other elements inert. ```javascript const dialog = document.getElementById("myDialog"); dialog.showModal(); ``` ### `.close([returnValue])` - Closes the dialog and optionally sets a return value. ```javascript const dialog = document.getElementById("myDialog"); dialog.close("User clicked OK"); ``` ### Conclusion Incorporating these built-in methods will enhance Bootstrap's dialog components in terms of usability and accessibility. ### Motivation and context I love bootstrap and have used it for years. Anything I can do to make it smaller. =)

title: [Docs] Link old pages to new ones
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

I would like to suggest that docs for older versions of Bootstrap include links to the newest versions.

### Motivation and context

This is a fast and convenient way for users to be able to access the latest versions of docs. It is especially useful when trying to search for a topic via a search engine, where users are often directed to articles for older Bootstrap versions. If their framework's major version number is not listed in the dropdown, they must either specify the desired version number in the search term, or navigate to getbootstrap.com's homepage and search there. This is unintuitive.
author: asummers1

title: Feature request: add responsive btn-group-vertical classes
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

Add responsive `btn-group-*` and/or `btn-group-*-vertical` classes, that cause a `btn-group` to appear vertical or horizontal at the specified breakpoints (`sm`, `md`, etc.).

### Motivation and context

`btn-group` is a handy component for visually/connecting multiple buttons. However, if there are lots of buttons with text (especially when using `btn-group-lg`), then the buttons will be awkwardly squished together.

For example, here is a 3-button group on a widescreen laptop:

![image](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/assets/8388785/54e0db71-0e13-4f27-847e-7ed50af5d4c4)

and again on a mobile screen:

![image](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/assets/8388785/e8e7b371-d638-4432-a0f0-3587c45261dc)

[`btn-group-vertical`](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/components/button-group/#vertical-variation) looks much nicer on mobile, but leads to a lot of unnecessary surrounding whitespace on widescreen:

![image](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/assets/8388785/7ac7d38d-abd2-4f99-850f-a8bbedf876b4)

This [SO answer](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/49176385/make-btn-group-responsive) with over 70 upvotes suggests using `btn-group flex-wrap`. However, this makes the button corners look weird on mobile without several additional `border-radius-*` utilities:

![image](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/assets/8388785/dddacd2e-232e-4201-b521-6417312dfbdb)

I could also use [block buttons](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/components/buttons/#block-buttons), but they lack the nice connected borders of a button group.

Long story short, if I could just put `btn-group-vertical btn-group-md` on my button groups so that they start vertical on mobile and then expand to horizontal on `md` breakpoints and above, it would make everything so much easier.

**Edit**: I know the proposed classes would conflict with the existing `btn-group-lg` and `btn-group-sm` classes for sizing... I don't have a great solution to suggest for that, other than making a breaking change or introducing more verbose classes like `btn-group-sm-horizontal`.

title: Bootstrap online builder tool
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

Unfortunately, because of bad developers who misused bootstrap, it became a sort of troll between developers. “Ah do you use bootstrap for that? lol” “If your site is slow it’s because of bootstrap” etc.

Indeed, there were and are quite a few developers who loaded the entire bootstrap just to stylize a button.

Many developers don't take the time to load only the SCSS or JS files they need, they load all the compressed CSS and JS for a simple trick.

My idea is: Create a tool on the bootstrap site (and maybe a sort of bundler later) that allows you to choose which component you want to use, for example: Navbar, Button, and/or Badge, and then we offer you which files you should call automatically.

Or as a bundler, CLI or something similar who detects our HTML and loads related JS, CSS etc.

Or we can add on each component documentation page the related files to load?

What do you think? If there are enough developers who agree with me and especially if it risks being accepted by bootstrap I can volunteer to start

### Motivation and context

It will improve developer experience, websites performance and bootstrap image for all developers trolling us 😅

author: MuhammadArif7

title: Define --toast-spacing above .toast
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)
- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues

### Describe the issue

Bootstrap will apply spacing between toasts using the CSS selector `.toast-container > :not(:last-child)`.  However, because the value comes from a variable that is only defined for `.toast`, the spacing is lost when an intermediate container is required.

In my particular case, I interjected an intermediate container to apply Svelte transition animations.

### Reduced test cases

[CodePen repro](https://codepen.io/webJose/pen/PoXRREV)

### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Windows

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Chrome, Microsoft Edge

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

v5.2

title: Active style for checkbox buttons should also apply with checkboxes embedded in the label
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

In many frameworks, the use of ID's is not required.  Many developers, myself included, prefer to embed the checkbox or radio button inside the label (be it to the left or right of the label text) because the browser can associate the label to the input without the need of an ID.

This is how many of us like to do it because we create components in Svelte or similar, and having to randomly generate ID's is a nuisance we don't want.  To handle the element imperatively, we use references.

Anyway, the problem at hand is:  With this way of constructing checkboxes/radios, the "checked" visual style is not applied to the label.

### Reduced test cases

[Demo](https://codepen.io/webJose/pen/MWZVLLd)

v5.2

title: Minor docs bug/issue: example code blocks have unnecessary 30px right-margin
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

Example on: https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/forms/form-control/#example

screen: (red border added by me to highlight issue more clearly)
![image](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/assets/1212885/23b843d5-2c9b-4e19-b75d-99e7e1f40bd9)

The code block has a 30px right-margin. I assume this is because the CSS rule used:

```css
@media (min-width: 992px)
.highlight pre {
  margin-right: 1.875rem;
}
```

This CSS is also used for `.bd-code-snippet` where there is no preview above and the code doesn't want to get in the way of the copy to clipboard button so it makes sense there.

Perhaps a quick easy fix could be to overrule the above, just for the examples.

```css
.bd-example-snippet .highlight pre {
  margin-right: 0;
}
```

If you agree I'd be happy to make a quick PR.

### Reduced test cases

see above:

Windows

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Microsoft Edge

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

v5.3.2

title: Use bootstrap ScrollSpy with anchor using dots?
body:
### Prerequisites - [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests - [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Context In the development of the pydata-sphinx-theme we use extensively twitter bootstrap to build the UI of a Sphinx generated documentation. When documenting Python API, the Autodoc plugin (that reads the Python docstring and transform it into a documentation page) creates anchors for functions that correspond to the exact path f the method: "pandas.DataFrame.from_csv". These points seems to be incompatible with the twitter bootstrap ScrollSpy tool as they are not interpreted as correct anchors. For interested reader, see this thread for more context: https://github.com/pydata/pydata-sphinx-theme/issues/1435 ### reproducible example I created this small fiddle by adapting one of bootstrap example: https://codepen.io/12rambau/pen/rNovoyw ```html
Item 1 Item2 Item 3 Item 4

Item 1

...

Item 2

...

Item 3

...

Item 4

...

``` As you see it's simply not working as I replaced the target names by "item.1" instead of "item 1". Going down this issue, it seems related to how uri are parsed in bootstrap. Is there any will to support my use case ? if yes how can I help, if not do you see a workaround that would not imply using custom scrollspy ?
author: 12rambau

title: License code in documentation under the MIT-0 license
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

License code in the documentation under the [MIT-0](https://github.com/aws/mit-0/blob/master/MIT-0) license.

### Motivation and context

The term "substantial" seems ambiguous to me. By licensing the code in the documentation under the [MIT-0](https://github.com/aws/mit-0/blob/master/MIT-0) license, it's always fine to copy any amount of code without requiring to include the copyright notice and permission notice in "substantial" portions. This removes the necessity to discern if the copied code is a substantial and non-substantial portion.

title: Bootstrap FocusTrap utility is intercepting external events and throwing Uncaught RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded
body:
### Prerequisites - [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues - [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems - [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Describe the issue Bootstrap uses its custom FocusTrap utility to trap focus to a modal. Bootstrap internally uses activate and deactivate functions to trap focus within show and hide calls. The issue is that when the focus-trap/focus-trap package is used with Bootstrap modal, both Bootstrap modal and focus-trap/focus-trap package start competing to focus their own tabable elements and the following stack overflow exception is raised within Bootstrap javascript: ``` Uncaught RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded. at FocusTrap._handleFocusin (focustrap.js:102:1) at HTMLDocument. (focustrap.js:72:1) at HTMLDocument.handler (event-handler.js:98:1) at tryFocus (index.js:432:1) at HTMLDocument.checkFocusIn (index.js:716:1) at FocusTrap._handleFocusin (focustrap.js:102:1) at HTMLDocument. (focustrap.js:72:1) at HTMLDocument.handler (event-handler.js:98:1) at tryFocus (index.js:432:1) at HTMLDocument.checkFocusIn (index.js:716:1) ``` The issue can be reproduced by displaying a Bootstrap modal along with an HTML fragment using focus-trap/focus-trap instance activated. The culprit is the Event Handler with event named "focusin.bs.focustrap" which gets triggered when modal tabable element is not focused. Can Bootstrap FocusTrap utility be modified in a way that focustrap can be enabled or disabled at will. The react code to reproduce the issue is below: ```js import { useEffect, useRef } from "react"; import * as focusTrap from 'focus-trap'; import { Modal } from "bootstrap"; function MyReactComponent() { useEffect(() => { var element = document.getElementById("modal-id"); var modal = Modal.getOrCreateInstance(element); modal.show(); var element2 = document.getElementById("trapped"); var instance = focusTrap.createFocusTrap(element2); instance.activate(); instance.unpause(); }, []); return ( <>
TEST
TEST
); } export default MyReactComponent; ``` ### Reduced test cases I cannot workout codepen but code is shared above. The fix should make activate and deactivate functions for focus trap configurable such that we can enable disable disable focus trap. ### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on? Windows ### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on? Chrome ### What version of Bootstrap are you using? 5.3

title: Have .nav-underline bottom aligned inside a .navbar container
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

Currently, if you add `.nav-underline` to a `.navbar-nav`, the underline styling won't line up with the bottom-border of the `.navbar`. For example:

https://stackblitz.com/edit/zugc1m?file=index.html

It'd be great if the underline styling applied to each `.nav-link` was aligned with bottom of the `.navbar` container.

### Motivation and context

Most underlined styled navigation I see nowadays tends to align the underline styling with the bottom of a `.navbar`-like container.
author: cpsievert

title: Should the color mode expose a variable or event that can be used by third-party components?
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

Should a parameter of the current color mode value be exposed for use by other components?

And whether there should be an active event that can be monitored when the color mode of the page changes.

### Motivation and context

For example, when using other components on the page, third-party components also support bright mode and other mode switching, and can be modified synchronously with the bootstrap color mode status. Should bootstrap provide convenience for this?
author: ZhangChengLin

title: navbars-offcanvas uses translateY to cause abnormal page width performance
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue



https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/examples/navbars-offcanvas/

transform: translateY(-150%);

In the above example, there are three navigation bars. In any one of them, adding the above style to the nav.navbar element will cause the page width to be abnormal. The specific manifestation is that there will be an extra blank area on the right side of the page, both on PC and mobile. Exceptions will occur. This is the smallest example imaginable.

It is very easy to reproduce, but it is not easy to describe clearly. You only need to add the transform style at the specified position. The specific value is not important, it will cause an exception. The following also uses offcanvas, but it will not cause this error.



The following official example shows no abnormal behavior

https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/examples/offcanvas-navbar/


Do you know this problem exists?


### Reduced test cases

![image](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/assets/30773389/4ce891ee-62e8-440f-8717-5bc31ac2cd7d)


![image](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/assets/30773389/6e8272fb-675a-4a48-8534-df6b7743037f)

Android

title: Blockquotes are not styled and do not nest anymore.
body:
### Prerequisites - [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues - [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems - [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Describe the issue In Bootstrap 3, blockquotes could be nested, and appear to be nested: e.g. `
Some quote here
A response here
` or
Some quote here
and
Some quote here
A response here
Also in bootstrap 5 blockquotes also use figure and figcaption. Again it would be good to have a way to nest these. e.g. ``` ``` or Styling was on the actual element. In Bootstrap 5, the styling has moved to a class, which I understand, but also any styling around this appears to have gone (indentation/padding, border colour etc). Furthermore, It is not clear form your documentation how to nest block quotes with bootstrap 5 with out overriding with some custom css e.g. ``` .blockquote { border-left: var(--bs-blockquote-border-width) solid var(--bs-body-color); font-style: italic; margin-left: 0; margin-right: 0; overflow: hidden; padding-left: var(--bs-blockquote-padding); padding-right: var(--bs-blockquote-padding); } ``` ### Reduced test cases 1. Update documentation and show how to indent/nest blockquotes 2. Bring back some elementary styling ### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on? macOS ### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on? Safari ### What version of Bootstrap are you using? 5.3.2

### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

My proposal is to reevaluate the passed options to the `Modal` constructor when `show()` is called.

A perfect working example of this is how Tooltip handles certain properties, like `placement` or `title`.
```js
{
  "placement": () => this.placement,
  "title": () => this.title
}
```

I'd like to see similar behaviour for the modal, like so:
```js
{
  "backdrop": () => this.shouldShowBackdrop,
  "focus": () => this.shouldFocus,
  "keyboard": () => this.allowKeyboard,
}
```

### Motivation and context

We're creating a modal using its constructor early in the runtime of the application. Then at later points in time, we pass dynamic content to the modal and then call show. The only problem with this approach is that we can't change any of the configuration initially passed into the constructor, like `keyboard`, once the modal instance was created.

title: Ability to disable `EventHandler` automatic listener assignments
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

Hey,

I was wondering why my uninitialized elements in my code — provided they have the data tags in place — get automatically initialized with the bootstrap modules I include somewhere else in my JS/TS code.

Then, after some debugging and tracing I figured it out: it's the `EventHandler.on` calls on the bottom of respective bootstrap module code.

I'd like to turn this off, but by looking at the code, right now I have no possibility of doing so.

The proposal is to be able to specify bootstrap that I will manually initialize my elements, and Bootstrap should not automatically do so by listening for various events on the document node.

### Motivation and context

I am using webpack and typescript to bundle my modules together using the latest bootstrap with vanilla JS. Problem is, once I include one Bootstrap module somewhere (Modal or Collapse are fine examples of this), it automatically starts listening on elements that I didn't even specify it to listen on. This makes development a bit frustrating, as I like to have full control over where I initialize modules.

I don't want Bootstrap modules to take over elements I don't specifically specify. Testing gets really frustrating when I want to see the places I forgot to initialize stuff, and I don't want it to automatically happen by code I have no control over.

body:

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues

### Describe the issue

Search google for "anything" related to Bootstrap. Even make the effort and add in `Bootstrap 5`, just to be sure you are not picking up Bootstrap 3, 4 ...

Find results, and - even if I develop almost daily - always realise you are probably on the _wrong_ documentation.

There is https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/, which has a "latest" option to choose from.
There is https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.2/, which has a "latest" option to choose from, but it is _not_ the latest! Above is! Yet the darn thing features the same "older" versions as the 5.3 doc does! 

(And many more other doc pages, all for a bit of version)

I would suggest, please, pretty please, put that stuff onto one page, make it default to the _latest_ first and open and active, and the user can then select _older_, _any older_ from the dropdown - without having to have nightmares to doubt if they are or not on the right doc.

This is really horrific.

Thank you.

Navigate the web. Search for Bootstrap.

title: Form Control input type="search" 'x' (clear input) button
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

The browser's built-in 'x' (clear input) button on input `type="search"` (in light mode) is a blue color in Chrome/Edge which doesn't quite match the look of Bootstrap - and likely many sites that use it. I suggest making a small CSS change to improve the look of it with this:

```css
input[type='search']::-webkit-search-cancel-button {
  filter: grayscale(1);
}
```

Demo:
https://stackblitz.com/edit/form-control-search-clear-gray-btn?file=index.html

### Motivation and context

With this change the clear input button will appear in gray which looks more like it's part of Bootstrap and I think a more neutral, natural color. Around the web it seems that gray is the most common color for a clear input x icon.

In dark mode the close button would be unchanged (it appears in white both with and without the above CSS).

I've tested this in both Edge 118 and Chrome 118. Note that Safari and Firefox already use gray for the close button.

### Proposal

A list everyone could contribute to, that collects useful libraries that extend Bootstraps functionality, like new components.

Some ideas:
- https://github.com/orchidjs/tom-select for custom select elements
- https://github.com/Eonasdan/tempus-dominus for Date/time picker widget
- https://github.com/lekoala/bootstrap5-autocomplete for autocomplete component
- https://github.com/lekoala/bootstrap5-tags for selectable tags
- https://github.com/dealfonso/power-buttons for confirmation dialogs and more
- https://github.com/kartik-v/bootstrap-star-rating for star rating component
and there are probably many more!

### Motivation and context

Many features that these libraries above provide are currently not implemented in Bootstrap, and probably won't be because of the work that comes with maintaining so many features.
Providing a list of good working libraries/plugins that fill those missing features would be very helpful to all Bootstrap users and could close some issues on this repo...

title: CSS variable consistancy
body:
### Prerequisites - [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests - [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Proposal I am currently working on a Figma design to incorporate variable-based theme support for a BS5-based site. During the process, I found the [CSS Variable documentation](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/customize/css-variables/) to be particularly helpful. I understand that the Bootstrap team invested significant time and effort in determining these names, likely with good reasons for maintaining the status quo. However, I firmly believe that constructive feedback is always beneficial, hence I would like to share my thoughts. ## Expectations To begin with, I have certain expectations from a variable collection: - Conciseness should be prioritized, with elements omitted if uncertain. - Clear and distinct intended purposes for each variable. - Semantic naming should be followed, focusing on the function rather than appearance. - Consistency across both light and dark themes. - Completeness: There should be no other "magic" colors in the CSS to allow seamless switching between light and dark themes by only replacing the variable definitions. - Bonus: Semantic grouping through the use of prefixes like `status-`, `text-`, `bg-` would be beneficial when sorting alphabetically, though I recognize this may be a personal preference. While developing the variable specification, I found the current naming conventions rather challenging to work with. As a result, I created a mapping table to prevent any errors. I am sharing my findings with the hope that they might be considered for inclusion in a future version of Bootstrap. ## Suggestions - Align definitions for dark and light themes for completeness and consistency. - Remove the `--bs-gray-XXX` variables, as their inversion for the dark theme could lead to misleading names. A potential alternative could be naming it `--bs-foreground-???`, where `--bs-foreground-full` represents the pure color for the light theme and `--bs-foreground-000` represents the background color. (Although there might be better names for `foreground`). - Remove all duplicate occurrences of `-rgb`. It's extremely unfortunate that CSS does not allow RGBA variables to be used in rules like...
`background-color: rgba(var(--bs-danger-rgb),var(--bs-bg-opacity))`
But I strongly believe that placing the burden on the theme-developer is confusing, adds unnecessary duplication, is error-prone, and bloats the size of the variable collections. I would prefer to enforce all variable definitions as rgb(23,23,23) as most of them don't use transparency anyways. - Replace the `-color` suffix with `-text` to avoid confusion and maintain consistency with variables such as `--bs-warning-text-emphasis` or `--bs-border-color`. - Replace `-light` and `-dark` with alternative wording. My suggestion is to use terms like `-muted` and `-invert`. - I think the definition of emphasizing levels like `subtle`, `(normal)`, `emphasis`, should be orthogonal to signals like `warning`, `success`, and usage `text`, `bg`, `border`. I can see how it can be nice for things like defining rules like `.alert-danger{}`. I just would have preferred separate variables for default `-text` and `-bg` colors to clarify that `--bs-danger` is never used for text and `-emphasize` is actually the default. If there is a `--bs-secondary-bg`, shouldn't there be `--bs-danger-bg`? I have the feeling that the naming for layout colors (e.g. `--bs-secondary-rgb`) collides with levels for signals like `--bs-secondary-bg-subtle`. I propose the following mapping: | BS5 | Proposal | Remark | | ---------------------------- | --------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- | | `--bs-primary` | `--bs-primary-fill` | Colored button background used with white text | | `--bs-primary-text-emphasis` | `--bs-primary-text` | Colored text in alert boxes, etc | | `--bs-primary-bg-subtle` | `--bs-primary-bg` | Background for alert boxes with colored text | | `--bs-primary-border-subtle` | `--bs-primary-border` | Outline for buttons and boxes | | `--bs-primary-bg` | (removed) | Either remove or clarify usage with a suffix like `-table-bg` | | `--bs-body-bg` | `--bs-bg-body` | Separate *layout* from *signal*. | | `--bs-secondary-rgb` | `--bs-bg-secondary` | makes it more relatable to *layout*. | - I initially missed variables for input-components, like `input-fill`, `input-border`, `input-placeholder` and states like `focused`, `required`, `disabled`. But after checking the source-code of the [Button-example](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/components/buttons/), I noticed that there actually is a variable `--bs-btn-disabled-color` and the documentation was just incomplete. A link to the complete list would have helped me. - If there is `--bs-emphasis-color` shouldn't there be a `--bs-subtle-color`? I would found that more intuitive than `secondary`. ## Proposal (RFC). For my project, I have created the following mapping. If things go well, I can release the figma-library with the correct mapping for dev export. | Figma | BS5 | Default dark | | ----------------------- | ------------------------------ | ------------------------- | | text/body | --bs-body-color | #dee2e6 | | text/emphasis | --bs-emphasis-color | #fff | | text/secondary | --bs-secondary-color | rgba(222, 226, 230, 0.75) | | text/tertiary | --bs-tertiary-color | rgba(222, 226, 230, 0.5) | | text/code | --bs-code-color | #e685b5 | | text/highlight | --bs-highlight-color | #dee2e6 | | bg/border-translucent | --bs-border-color-translucent | rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.15) | | bg/body | --bs-body-bg | #212529 | | bg/border | --bs-border-color | #495057 | | bg/highlight | --bs-highlight-bg | #664d03 | |
