- [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal


Extend the margin and padding capabilities as required by the programmer. Create a class that can be set in px, percentage, or viewport as required.

mt-[300px] // margin-top 300 px
p-[25%] // all sides 25%

### Motivation and context

This would improve Bootstrap's ability to have customizable elements within components regarding spacing.
author: aisakk

title: Separate dist files for components
body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

Hello everybody!

I searched for duplicate and didn't found any but I may have used terms that are too generic, sorry if I'm pushing something that has been openly discussed elsewhere.

I'm wondering if the different component asset files (css and js) could be generated as separate dist files.
Being part of the Drupal community I see many projects that are recompiling those separately to allow an optimized aggregation of the css and js files. Here are some examples:

- https://git.drupalcode.org/project/bootstrap/-/tree/5.0.x/components/accordion?ref_type=heads
- https://git.drupalcode.org/project/bootstrap_barrio/-/tree/5.5.x/components/accordion?ref_type=heads
- https://git.drupalcode.org/project/bootstrap5/-/tree/4.0.x/css/components?ref_type=heads

### Motivation and context

Many CMSs or frameworks often have a process that aggregate CSS/JS files to be sure to ship only assets that are in use on a page rather than loading all components even if you don't use most/many of them.

As a company we have more and more clients that are asking us to ensure a certain ecoIndex score or similar ecodesign scores that check how efficiently the assets are loaded and used. Having them separately would greatly help in aggregation processes to optimize scores on such tools.
author: Nicoloye

title: Suggestion: Online tool to download copy-pastable style declarations with all CSS vars resolved
body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

I'm suggesting an online tool that lets you enter a single class name (e.g. `.card`), and then returns the full declaration of that class *with all CSS `var(--...)` resolved to their actual values*.

### Motivation and context

Sometimes it's not handy (or possible) to include the entire bootstrap.css file, e.g. I just recommended to include just the needed CSS classes for PDF creation with dompdf: https://github.com/dompdf/dompdf/wiki/Common-Use-Cases#using-css-frameworks-like-bootstrap-or-tailwind

title: Accessibility Improvement: Close buttons (for alerts, modals etc)
body: One thing I always have in my mind when building accessible components is 'the better this works without any CSS the better', (and 'the better this works without JavaScript the better') so from time to time I disable one or the other and see if any improvements can be made. With that in mind, when working on a standard Bootstrap alert (with dismiss button) from the example code I noticed it appears like this with CSS disabled: ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/aeef357c-5f69-4858-a41f-40462e44569e) Because the `.btn-close` is like this: ```html ``` I think this could be improved with the following: ```html ``` So it appears like this: ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/28e54387-d44d-41e8-ad0b-61633bd492ef) it does mean a few more characters/bytes of markup added but I believe it's worth it. it makes the content more resilient and usable even if CSS for any reason fails to load. Also, [aria-labels are not always auto-translated](https://adrianroselli.com/2019/11/aria-label-does-not-translate.html) for users reading the content in a different language, but using a visually hidden text label would be translated - another potential accessibility benefit. if you agree, I'd be happy to open a PR to update the relevant examples where `aria-label="Close"` is used.

title: Sass Deprecation Warning: green() is deprecated in Bootstrap SCSS with Vite
body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [x] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

While migrating my React project from CRACO to Vite, I encountered a Sass deprecation warning originating from Bootstrap’s SCSS source files.

The issue appears due to Vite using Dart Sass, which strictly enforces new syntax rules. Specifically, Bootstrap is using the deprecated green() color function in its internal _functions.scss file.

Environment
Project Tooling: React + Vite
Bootstrap Version: 5.2.3
Dart Sass Version: 1.63.0 (latest at time of testing)
Vite Version: 5.x
Node Version: >=18

### Reduced test cases

Deprecation Warning [color-functions]: green() is deprecated. Suggestion:

color.channel($color, "green", $space: rgb)

More info: https://sass-lang.com/d/color-functions

   ╷
37 │   @return red($value), green($value), blue($value);
   │                        ^^^^^^^^^^^^^
   ╵
    node_modules/bootstrap/scss/_functions.scss 37:24   to-rgb()

Bootstrap should migrate away from deprecated Sass functions like red(), green(), and blue() and use the recommended color.channel() instead, as per Dart Sass guidelines. The warning is triggered from the Bootstrap SCSS inside node_modules, which makes it difficult to fix manually since modifying files in node_modules is not reliable or recommended—it can vary between environments and gets overwritten on install. Is there a recommended approach or plan to update these usages in a future Bootstrap release?

### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Windows

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Chrome

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

 5.2.3

title: Tapping form-floating input doesn't scroll to input when touch keyboard opens on some Android and iOS devices
body:
### Prerequisites - [x] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues - [x] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems - [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Describe the issue **Issue:** On some devices, such as the Google Pixel 8 running the latest Chrome, Bootstrap’s floating label inputs do not scroll into view when the touch keyboard opens, even when the input is hidden underneath the keyboard. I've also had reports of the same issue on iPhone 12 pro/Safari/iOS 18.5, although I am unable to recreate. **Expected behaviour:** When a user taps on an input field, the mobile browser should scroll the input into view so it is not obscured by the on-screen keyboard. This is standard behaviour on both Android and iOS for native and web inputs. **Observed behaviour:** When using Bootstrap’s .form-floating markup, the input remains hidden behind the keyboard on certain devices (like Pixel 8 with Chrome), and the page does not scroll to reveal the focused input. This occurs even on the official Bootstrap docs example page: https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.0/forms/floating-labels/ **Example markup from that page:** ```html
``` **Reproduction steps:** 1. Open the following link on a Pixel 8 in Chrome: https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.0/forms/floating-labels/ 2. Scroll down so the input is near the bottom of the screen 3. Tap the input 4. The keyboard opens, but the input is not scrolled into view. **This behaviour can be intermittent.** 5. Typing a character then causes it to scroll into view (delayed reflow) **Possible cause:** It may be related to the internal use of position: absolute, transform, and transition effects inside .form-floating, which may affect how Chrome calculates whether the input is already visible. **Environment:** Device: Pixel 8 OS: Android 14 Browser: Chrome 114+ Bootstrap Version: 5.0 ### Reduced test cases You can recreate the issue using a Google Pixel 8 running Chrome at: https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.0/forms/floating-labels/ ### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on? Windows, Android ### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on? Chrome ### What version of Bootstrap are you using? 5.0

title: [offcanvas] - triggers unexpected scroll to top when scroll-padding-top is set on html, body
body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [x] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

When using an offcanvas component, closing it (either via the close button or by clicking on the backdrop) unexpectedly scrolls the page to the top — but **only when** the following CSS rule is applied:

```css
html, body {
  scroll-padding-top: 100px;
}
```
This seems to be a Chromium-specific issue.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:

1. Add scroll-padding-top: 100px; to html, body
2. Add an offcanvas with a toggle and a close button
3. Scroll the page down
4. Open the offcanvas
5. Click on the close button or the backdrop to dismiss it
6. Observe the page jumps back to the top

**Expected behavior**
The page scroll position should not change when closing an offcanvas, regardless of scroll-padding-top.

**Environment**
- Bootstrap version: 5.3.7, 5.3.2 
- Browser: 
  - ✅ **Firefox**: Works correctly, no scroll jump
  - ❌ **Chrome** (v125+) and **Edge** (v125+): Scrolls to top when closing offcanvas
- OS: Windows


Removing scroll-padding-top fixes the issue: the scroll position remains unchanged after closing the offcanvas.

### Reduced test cases

Codepen => https://codepen.io/David-Henriet/pen/GgpqLrm

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d9aaa96a-cb55-4aff-9043-24719159a146

Windows

Chrome

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?
5.3.7, 5.3.2

title: Keyboard navigation for Tabs and Tab Dropdown Menues
body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [x] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

I am experiencing a possible accessibility issue involving keyboard navigation when using Tabs combined with Tab menus. When a tab has focus the roving tab index sets the other tabs to have tabindex="-1" so that they are skipped when pressing `Tab`. However, this does not happen for Tab Menus, and pressing `Tab` while on a Tab will skip from the current Tab to the Tab Menu, and `Shift+Tab` will move back to the last focused tab. `Left` and `Right` arrow keys successfully navigate between Tabs, but once a Tab Menu is focused, arrow keys do not seem to allow one to navigate back to Tabs. Meanwhile if one has multiple tab menus, arrow keys do not navigate between them and one must use `Tab` or `Shift+Tab` to navigate between tab menus. This inconsistent keyboard navigation, and conflicting when using them in combination, seems like an accessibility issue.

title: [docs] Carousel `to` API incorrect (v4)
body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [x] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

> .carousel('to')
> Cycles the carousel to a particular frame (0 based, similar to an array). Returns to the caller before the next item has been shown (i.e. before the slid.bs.carousel event occurs).

https://getbootstrap.com/docs/4.6/components/carousel/#carouselto

It's difficult to determine what is meant by this. Probably one of the more obvious assumptions might be that the API is `.carousel('to', 0)`.

Then this page shows (incorrect) examples:

```
$('#myCarousel').carousel('1')
```

https://getbootstrap.com/docs/4.6/getting-started/javascript/#asynchronous-functions-and-transitions

This will result in unknown method errors.

...

Instead the undocumented approach of `$('#myCarousel').carousel(1)` must be used. Somehow Google search AI summary knew about this.

When recognising a number being passed it will jump to that frame.

title: Modal opened from dropdown has focused close button
body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [x] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

The focus behavior of a modal depends on whether or not the toggle element is inside a dropdown or not.

When the element with `data-bs-toggle="modal"` is in a dropdown, the close button in the modal header is focused when the modal is shown.

If the toggle element is not in a dropdown, the modal itself receives focus (as expected)

### Reduced test cases

https://codepen.io/bigdogwillfeed/pen/vENjJKM

Pretty much straight from the example docs, aside from the red background on focused elements.

If you open the modal with the first button, the modal is focused (as expected)
If you open the modal with the button inside the dropdown, the close button in the header is focused

### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

macOS and Windows

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Firefox and Chrome

5.3.7

title: v5.2+ regression: default popover header styles missing
body: ### Prerequisites - [x] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues - [x] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems - [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Describe the issue When upgrading between Bootstrap v5.1 to v5.2, the default styles for the `.popover-header` class appear to be missing. Upon closer inspection, it appears that the new CSS variables are not set even though default variables are assigned in the popover stylesheet: https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/b8880e5eec6bb4f33578578ba2413f0d91424382/scss/_popover.scss#L1-L23 Looking at the code, I think the problem might be that the variables are assigned in the `.popover` block, but `.popover-header` is defined outside of that block towards the bottom of the file. The other classes outside of the `.popover` block that refer to those variables then might also be affected. ### Reduced test cases v5.1: https://codepen.io/Grant-Bourque/pen/gbaddPz v5.2 (same behavior as current latest): https://codepen.io/Grant-Bourque/pen/ByoOOQq v5.1 next to v5.2: ### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on? macOS ### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on? Firefox ### What version of Bootstrap are you using? v5.3.8

title: Support for viewing containers on UHD displays
body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

Using the `container` class on things is very popular and useful, but on displays wider than 1400 pixels, it leaves ever increasing amounts of whitespace. [Doc link to containers](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/layout/containers/)

On a 3240px wide display, the amount of space is almost 2/3 of the screen size.

A similar issue has been reported before: #22000

I'm not sure if this is a bug or feature request, so guidance is appreciated.

Thank you for your work.

### Motivation and context

Since larger 4k screens are available and practical to use in their native resolution without scaling, it would be nice to either have more "stops" so containers can grow wider, or increase the stops.

I'm not sure what is best, but the current state is a lot of wasted space, and zooming the webpage so it fits the screen makes the font too big.

title: <button> resets border-radius while button <input>s don't
body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [x] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

Bootstrap's Reboot module contains the following:

```scss
// Remove the default `border-radius` that macOS Chrome adds.
// See https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues/24093

button {
  // stylelint-disable-next-line property-disallowed-list
  border-radius: 0;
}
```

However, it only targets the `button` element, and not `input` elements that are buttons.

I believe there are two solutions to this issue:
- Either add `input[type="button"]`, `input[type="submit"]`, and `input[type="reset"]` to the selector, or
- Remove it altogether, undoing #24093. When using Bootstrap's button classes, `border-radius` gets replaced anyway.

### Reduced test cases

- A test case exists on Bootstrap's own website: https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/content/reboot/#forms
- https://codepen.io/c-ezra-m/pen/VYvgdmb

These were inspected in Waterfox, a fork of Firefox.

### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Windows

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Firefox

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

v5.3.8

title: color-contrast found no color leads to compilation failure
body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [x] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

I am using Webpack to compile Bootstrap. Everything works just fine. But when inside a Docker container based on image `node-24.8-bookworm-slim` the compilation fails because `color-contrast` found "no color leading to 4:1 contrast ratio against #0d6efd".

That warning leads to this compilation error:

```
24.36 $color1: null is not a color.
24.36     ╷
24.36 846 │ $btn-link-focus-shadow-rgb:   to-rgb(mix(color-contrast($link-color), $link-color, 15%)) !default;
24.36     │                                      ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
24.36     ╵
```

I am on a Mac with M2 chip and ` platform: linux/x86_64` defined in the compose file.

Tried to hardcode `$btn-link-focus-shadow-rgb: 0,0,0 !default` but then compilation stucks at ~40% with no error message.

### Reduced test cases

Followed the Webpack docs to setup webpack here's the dockerfile

```
FROM --platform=linux/amd64 docker.io/node:24.8-bookworm-slim AS client-builder

ARG APP_HOME=/app
WORKDIR ${APP_HOME}

COPY ./package.json ${APP_HOME}
RUN npm install && npm cache clean --force

COPY . ${APP_HOME}
RUN npm run build
```


### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

macOS

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Firefox

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

5.3.8

author: tuannvbg

title: v6: Swap from `@use` to `@forward`
body:
### Discussed in https://github.com/orgs/twbs/discussions/41748
Originally posted by **mrholek** September 16, 2025 Hi @mdo 👋 First of all, thank you for all the work on v6-dev; it looks really promising. I write here because the conversation in PR has been locked and limited to collaborators. I have a question/suggestion about how variables are exposed in `scss/bootstrap.scss`, and how consumers can override them. #### What I noticed In `bootstrap.scss`, the components and utility styles are included directly (via `@use`) rather than being re-exported through an **entry-point** that could allow variable overriding by the consumer via `with(...)`. For example, the structure is roughly: ```scss // bootstrap.scss ... @use "alert"; ... ``` This approach means consumers **cannot** override component variables (like padding, color, etc.) at the moment of `@use 'bootstrap' with (…);` because `bootstrap.scss` doesn't `@forward` those component variables. #### Why `@forward` might be useful Using `@forward` in `bootstrap.scss` allows users to override defaults, e.g.: ```scss @use "bootstrap" with ( $alert-margin-bottom: 3rem, $alert-link-font-weight: 800 ); ```

title: Session lost with Chrome and DevTools opened
body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [x] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

Hello,
With the latest version of Chrome, if I open the development tools with F12, the post will lose my session.
I have an MVC site built with C# Framework 4.7.2.
The incompatibility is with version 5.3.8 of the Boostrap bundle loaded locally. If I load it from the CDN, the problem doesn't occur. It's not caused by browser extensions, and the same problem occurs in Edge. The problem doesn't occur on other browsers under the same conditions.
I do not know if the problem is related to Bootstrap or Chrome.

### Reduced test cases

I have a page with jquery 3.7.1 and Bootstrap bundle min 5.3.8.
When I do a submit, in the controller side I lost my variable in the session.

### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Windows

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Chrome

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

5.3.8

### Proposal

Dear Sir or Madam,

I am working on a website but have run into a small problem. Hopefully, someone can help me or tell me how to solve this.I used an Accordion from Bootstrap 5.3, and I have customized it to some extent. Now, I want to remove the border-radius on the last row (last of type), but how do I do that? See the yellow circles.

Kind regards,John

It would be better to have everything in a single style...

body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

Hi My name is Jamell Anderson, I'm a worldpay corporate sales director and I'm trying to help a client reduce their current transaction fees by over 50%. 

This company are willing to change their website if needed, however I thought it would be beneficial for everyone if we could work together to ensure we can integrate out gateway without taking them away from your framework. 

If someone can please contact me as soon as possible as we have multiple clients all willing to stay with your framework if you can produce an integration.

I would prefer to work with you guys on this than go through the pain staking process of having them rebuild their website with woo commerce or another framework. 

Please contact me via my email or preferably call me today as all three are time sensitive. 

Kind Regards 

Jamell 

Email- Jamell.anderson@worldpay.com
Phone number- 07586058271

### Motivation and context

Prevent your current clients from changing their website provider, ensure easier process for their switch over to our gateway. 

Lastly ensure we can work with more clients using your framework in the future without having to get them to move over to a woo commerce fro example as we have the integration already in place

title: Clicks within forms within dropdowns no longer ignored and close the dropdown
body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [x] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

In bootstrap 4, the dropdown js was ignoring clicks on child elements of any form within the dropdown.

```
.on(EVENT_CLICK_DATA_API, SELECTOR_FORM_CHILD, e => {
    e.stopPropagation()
  })
```

https://stackblitz.com/edit/ei1szkk7-n9jbbfnv?file=index.html

In current bootstrap, this is no longer this case.  Only events from "input"s directly are ignored. This breaks functionality such as clicking on a form input element's label to focus the input.

```
// Tab navigation through the dropdown menu or events from contained inputs shouldn't close the menu
      if (context._menu.contains(event.target) && ((event.type === 'keyup' && event.key === TAB_KEY) || /input|select|option|textarea|form/i.test(event.target.tagName))) {
        continue
      }
```

https://stackblitz.com/edit/ei1szkk7?file=index.html

I think it would also be helpful if the dropdown documentation page had an example with a form within an actual dropdown (with toggle) rather than just within a "dropdown-menu" element.

### Reduced test cases

https://stackblitz.com/edit/ei1szkk7?file=index.html

### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Windows

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Chrome

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

5.3

title: Navigation links have no active appearance inside Nav components
body:
### Prerequisites - [x] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues - [x] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems - [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Describe the issue There is no contribution to the hyperlink's style from the `active` class, as verified by the browser's developer tools. This can be seen in the official documentation: [Navs and Tabs](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/components/navs-tabs/) Just locate the link labeled **Active**, and inspect it. Now browse the list of classes in the Style tab in developer tools. It can be seen that `active` is not contributing anything. ### Reduced test cases Taken from the documentation, this is the first example: ```html
  • Active
  • Link
  • Link
  • Disabled

title: Production build should not reference sourcemaps
body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [x] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

The production builds reference sourcemaps due to trailing comments.

`bootstrap.bundle.min.js`:
```
//# sourceMappingURL=bootstrap.bundle.min.js.map
```
`bootstrap.min.css`:
```
/*# sourceMappingURL=bootstrap.min.css.map */
```
That unfortunately spams the devtools console on every page load. Example from my dev machine::
```
Source map error: Error: request failed with status 404
Resource URL: http://localhost:80/bootstrap.min.css
Source Map URL: bootstrap.min.css.map
```
The production build should not have source map support, because it's unnecessary, it spams the console and generates significantly more traffic. (CDN-served assets also have sourcemaps in production builds.)

### Reduced test cases

- Reference minified css and js files, either from local sources or CDN
- Open devtools console - errors
- Measure traffic - much more traffic as those map files are huge

### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Linux, iOS, Android, Windows, macOS

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Firefox

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

v5.3.6

title: Empty form-floating fields without "placeholder" attribute always have a floating label
body:
### Prerequisites - [x] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues - [x] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems - [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Describe the issue If you don't define the attribute "placeholder" at all, the label always floats ````
```` Same happens for an `` element by the way. It is this css causing it: ```` .form-floating > .form-control:not(:placeholder-shown) ~ label { transform: scale(.85) translateY(-.5rem) translateX(.15rem) } ```` Then another thing is that when you add the placeholder attribute. The label is not transformed anymore (while it should because we now have a placeholder) but the placeholder itself is not showing up anywhere. This last thing might be already mentioned here #36023 ### Reduced test cases Change ```` .form-floating > .form-control:not(:placeholder-shown) ~ label { transform: scale(.85) translateY(-.5rem) translateX(.15rem) } ```` to ```` .form-floating > .form-control:placeholder-shown ~ label { transform: scale(.85) translateY(-.5rem) translateX(.15rem) } ```` Probably some more css has this same issue where you should invert `:not(:placeholder-shown)` to `:placeholder-shown` ### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on? macOS ### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on? Chrome ### What version of Bootstrap are you using? v5.3.7

title: v6 should use light-dark() for colors.
body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

Make use of `light-dark()` in v6.

```css
	--body-bg-color: light-dark(
		oklch(0.6324 0.1801 var(--base-hue)),
		oklch(0.2176 0.0987 var(--base-hue))
	);
```

Playground: https://codepen.io/nnico/pen/VYeMbLw

[It's baseline 2024](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Reference/Values/color_value/light-dark), we are going towards 2026, not sure when v6 is expected to be stable, but I think it should be considered fine.

### Motivation and context

Thinking out loud here. If I got it right, this would:

1. Eliminate the need for the `$color-mode-type: data;` thing. Currently, v5 needs a SCSS recompile to switch to `$color-mode-type: media-query;` for native media query color mode selection. But this kills all the nice stuff so it's not used by default. This would basically make v6 just **only** use native media query and have the best of both worlds.
2. Not saying that something works without JS is a huge argument for 2026 but still, it is the case that currently bootstrap by default (as the docs do), loads the HTML with `data-bs-theme=auto` and need JS to choose the right theme later. With `light-dark()` this all would feel more native and how It's supposed to be I think.
3. This would enable switching component themes (actual color themes) and dark mode **independently** of each other.
4. This could force every theme to provide dark and light mode colors with the theme. Actually, if the light and dark colors for the initial `light-dark()`s come from CSS vars themselves, dark and light theme could even be switched independently of each other globally.
5. Every color that is the result of a `light-dark()` can be thought of as single color, it can be manipulated with CSS.
6. Plays nicer with 3rd party code, what people expect in modern CSS.

title: Docs: warn about usability / accessibility issues of Toasts
body:
I was just reading GitHub's Primer Design system page on their decision to no longer uses Toasts (https://primer.style/accessibility/toasts/) and it's put me off from ever using them :-) . There are many accessibility and usability issues which I hadn't previously considered.

I know Bootstrap does a great job in making it's components as accessible as possible and provides helpful accessibility docs where necessary. I think it could be a good idea to highlight some of the shortcomings, perhaps more info in the [Accessibility section](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/components/toasts/#accessibility) or a warning/info callout at the top suggesting to consider using a different component/pattern?

author: Sooooo7

title: [Feature Request] Add prefers-reduced-motion support for all carousel animations
body:
## Description
While Bootstrap supports `prefers-reduced-motion` for some animations, it would be helpful to ensure comprehensive coverage across all carousel transition effects.

## Current Behavior
Some carousel animations may still play even when users have enabled reduced motion preferences in their OS settings.

## Proposed Enhancement
- Audit all carousel CSS transitions and JavaScript animations
- Ensure `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` is consistently applied
- Consider adding a JavaScript API option to programmatically disable animations

## Benefits
1. **Accessibility**: Helps users with vestibular disorders who experience motion sickness
2. **WCAG Compliance**: Aligns with WCAG 2.1 Success Criterion 2.3.3
3. **User Experience**: Respects user preferences set at the OS level

## Environment
- Bootstrap version: v5.3.x
- Browser: All modern browsers

title: Validation messages should not appear when the input field is empty
body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

For the `.was-validated .form-control:valid` and `.was-validated .form-control:invalid` selectors, I believe validation messages should not be shown when the input element has no content. It seems that Bootstrap uses the `:placeholder-shown` pseudo-class to determine whether the input has content.

### Motivation and context

Although I understand this design might be debatable, showing "invalid" feedback to a user the moment they open a page—before they type anything—is really not a good user experience.

I also understand that I can add `.was-validated` after there is user input or use `.is-valid` and `.is-invalid`, but my point is: if this simple task can be handled purely with CSS, then there's no need to use JavaScript.

This is the first nested item’s accordion body. It is shown by default, until the collapse plugin adds the appropriate classes that we use to style each element. These classes control the overall appearance, as well as the showing and hiding via CSS transitions. You can modify any of this with custom CSS or overriding our default variables. It’s also worth noting that just about any HTML can go within the .accordion-body, though the transition does limit overflow.

This is the second nested item’s accordion body. It is hidden by default, until the collapse plugin adds the appropriate classes that we use to style each element. These classes control the overall appearance, as well as the showing and hiding via CSS transitions. You can modify any of this with custom CSS or overriding our default variables. It’s also worth noting that just about any HTML can go within the .accordion-body, though the transition does limit overflow.

This is the third nested item’s accordion body. It is hidden by default, until the collapse plugin adds the appropriate classes that we use to style each element. These classes control the overall appearance, as well as the showing and hiding via CSS transitions. You can modify any of this with custom CSS or overriding our default variables. It’s also worth noting that just about any HTML can go within the .accordion-body, though the transition does limit overflow.

This is the second parent item’s accordion body. It is hidden by default, until the collapse plugin adds the appropriate classes that we use to style each element. These classes control the overall appearance, as well as the showing and hiding via CSS transitions. You can modify any of this with custom CSS or overriding our default variables. It’s also worth noting that just about any HTML can go within the .accordion-body, though the transition does limit overflow.

This is the third parent item’s accordion body. It is hidden by default, until the collapse plugin adds the appropriate classes that we use to style each element. These classes control the overall appearance, as well as the showing and hiding via CSS transitions. You can modify any of this with custom CSS or overriding our default variables. It’s also worth noting that just about any HTML can go within the .accordion-body, though the transition does limit overflow.
``` ### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on? Linux ### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on? Chrome ### What version of Bootstrap are you using? v5.3

title: Reboot: Accent color CSS
body:
### Prerequisites - [x] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests - [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Proposal This is a minor thing, but if you check the Forms section on Reboot (https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/content/reboot/#forms). The range slider, radio button and checkbox color is purple in Edge on Windows, darker blue on Firefox on Windows, Grey in Edge on macOS, blue on Safari on macOS. I think settings accent-color to `#0d6efd` would be useful.
Screenshots

Edge (Windows)

Firefox (Windows)

### Motivation and context The default Accent colors vary between browsers. I think setting it to be consistent would be helpful. I know this isn't too important because Bootstrap's own range slider, radio button and checkbox components already take care of this if you use the CSS classes... but there may be occasions where perhaps a radio button or checkbox is inserted into the DOM (via third-party) or something and having it be a consistent color at least would be helpful. Reboot is designed to normalize/standardize styles between browsers and this falls into that category.
author: coliff

title: Docs: Using the Speculation Rules API for faster page loads
body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

Bootstrap's docs are already very fast as they are static HTML, but page load performance can be further improved with a tiny snippet of code in the `<head>`. This only works in Chromium browsers at moment but I think it's a nice progressive enhancement. I use it on all sites I work on.

```html
<script type="speculationrules">
{
  "prerender": [{
    "where": {
      "href_matches": "/*"
    },
    "eagerness": "moderate"
  }]
}
</script>
```

Further reading:

- https://developer.chrome.com/docs/web-platform/prerender-pages
- https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Speculation_Rules_API
- https://caniuse.com/mdn-html_elements_script_type_speculationrules

### Motivation and context

Even faster docs!

Suggestion: if(sass($arg == "$value"): $value; else: $arg)

More info: https://sass-lang.com/d/if-function

Suggestion: if(sass($arg == "$key"): $key; else: if($arg == "$value", $value, $arg))

More info: https://sass-lang.com/d/if-function

   ╷
58 │       $_args: append($_args, if($arg == "$key", $key, if($arg == "$value", $value, $arg)));
   │                              ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
   ╵
    node_modules\bootstrap\scss\_functions.scss 58:30  @import
    Assets\scss\main.scss 5:9                          root stylesheet

Deprecation Warning [if-function]: The Sass if() syntax is deprecated in favor of the modern CSS syntax.

Suggestion: if(sass($l1 > $l2): divide($l1 + 0.05, $l2 + 0.05); else: divide($l2 + 0.05, $l1 + 0.05))

More info: https://sass-lang.com/d/if-function

More info: https://sass-lang.com/d/if-function

author: mwentz

title: [Popover] data-bs-content="true"/"false" auto-converted to boolean causes TypeError, but "true"/"false" are valid string content.
body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [x] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

When using data-bs-content="true" (or "false") on an element with data-bs-toggle="popover", Bootstrap internally converts the string "true" to the boolean true, causing _typeCheckConfig to throw:

`TypeError: POPOVER: Option "content" provided type "boolean" but expected type "(null|string|element|function)".`

This prevents users from displaying the literal string "true" or "false" — a common and valid use case (e.g., showing boolean-like values in tables, configs, JSON previews).

### Reduced test cases

1. Add this HTML:
`<button type="button" class="btn btn-secondary" data-bs-toggle="popover" data-bs-content="true">
  Show "true"
</button>`

2. Initialize popovers (e.g., via new bootstrap.Popover() or auto-init via data-bs-toggle).
3. Observe console error:
`TypeError: POPOVER: Option "content" provided type "boolean" but expected type "(null|string|element|function)".`

### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Windows

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Chrome

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

v5.2.1
author: Andyz0103

title: Add utility classes for placeholder text alignment (logical start/end)
body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

Add utility classes for aligning placeholder text independently from input text alignment, following Bootstrap’s logical property conventions.

This proposal introduces two new utility classes:

- `.placeholder-start`
- `.placeholder-end`

These utilities apply logical text alignment to the `::placeholder` pseudo-element:

- `start` → aligns placeholder text to the inline start edge
- `end` → aligns placeholder text to the inline end edge

The proposed behavior mirrors existing text utilities such as `text-start` and `text-end`, but targets placeholder text specifically.

Example usage:

```html
<input
  type="text"
  class="form-control text-start placeholder-end"
  placeholder="شماره تلفن"
/>
```
### Motivation and context

In real-world form designs—especially in RTL interfaces—it is common to require different alignment rules for placeholder text and user-entered content.

A typical example is numeric or code-based inputs:
- The input value must be left-aligned (for readability and consistency)
- The placeholder text may need to be visually aligned to the opposite edge to match surrounding UI expectations

While CSS fully supports this use case via the `::placeholder` pseudo-element, Bootstrap currently provides no utility-level solution to control placeholder alignment independently.

As a result, developers must fall back to custom CSS, which:
- Breaks consistency with Bootstrap’s utility-first approach
- Reduces reusability across projects
- Adds friction when working with mixed LTR/RTL layouts

This proposal aims to fill that gap by introducing a minimal, logical, and consistent utility API that:
- Aligns with Bootstrap’s existing `start` / `end` naming conventions
- Works seamlessly in both LTR and RTL contexts
- Improves real-world form UX without introducing physical (left/right) abstractions
- Avoids custom CSS for a common, valid use case

The proposed utilities are intentionally scoped, opt-in, and follow the same design principles as existing text and spacing utilities in Bootstrap.

title: Docs: Suggestion; update "Color and background" note on color-contrast function
body:

### Proposal

The 'Colors & background' page (https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/helpers/color-background/#overview ) note at the top says:

> "There’s currently no support for a CSS-native color-contrast function"

which was likely true at the time it was written, but perhaps that could be update to something like. 

> "There’s currently limited support for the contrast-color function"

with a link to: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Reference/Values/color_value/contrast-color

### Motivation and context

Just a bit more context for this. Safari 26 and Firefox 146 both already support contrast-color(), hopefully [Chrome/Edge will add support soon](https://issues.chromium.org/issues/40142548)!

title: Shift-Tab in modal causes background to scroll
body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [x] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

When creating and opening a modal via JavaScript, I am experiencing an oddity with keyboard navigation:

1. Open the modal (in my case it includes a close button in the heading)
2. Observe that using Tab to navigate places focus on the close button
3. Use Shift-Tab to navigate backwards.  The focus visibly stays on the close button as it's the first and only focusable element inside the modal, but the underlying page scrolls down to the bottom (probably because that's where the modal lies in the overall DOM)

Expected behavior:

* Using Shift-Tab does not cause body scrolling

Based on investigation it appears that focus temporarily moves to something in the main page before the focus trap resets it to the modal's close button.  Will post more detail here if I find it.


### Reduced test cases

See issue description

Windows

Microsoft Edge, Chrome

5.3.8

author: Zannick

title: Scrollable modal may clip overflowing elements on the left
body:
### Prerequisites - [x] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues - [x] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems - [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Describe the issue I am testing behavior of some modals that may have horizontal lists, whether actual horizontal list-groups (as in the repro case below) or a non-wrapping flex div of buttons. I'm using `justify-content-center` on the `list-group`/list element, which makes the list centered. When the modal is too small, I expected a) the list to be still centered initially, and b) the modal to be scrollable to the sides to see the overflowing content. What actually happens: a) the list is still centered ✔, but b) the modal can only be scrolled to the right, but not to the left ❌ (Alternatively, if a) the list was not centered ❌, but b) the modal could be scrolled to the overflowing content (to the right) ✔, this would be partially acceptable, because then the scroll problem can be solved with javascript. But prefer if bootstrap did that automatically.) ### Reduced test cases ```html
    A simple default list group item A simple primary list group item A simple secondary list group item A simple success list group item A simple danger list group item
``` ```css .list-group-item-action { min-width: 150px !important; } ``` Removing `justify-content-center` doesn't scroll to the modal margin but starts with the correct margin on the left: The only good workaround I've found is to put `min-width: fit-content` on an element inside the body, which has to be on a wrapper element if I have multiple lists or the lists won't all be centered: All these examples are in https://codepen.io/Zannick/pen/pvbNOGG ### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on? Windows ### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on? Chrome ### What version of Bootstrap are you using? v5.3.7

body: ### Prerequisites - [x] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues - [x] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems - [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Describe the issue **Test Environment:** OS: Windows 11 version 25H2 (OS Build 26220.7653) Browser: Edge (Version 144.0.3719.82) Tool: Color Contrast Analyzer Tool URL: https://codesandbox.io/p/devbox/epic-jasper-gz8pcz?file=%2findex.html&workspaceId=ws_MVpzSDr2nAm9FXcihgWukZ **User Impact:** Users with low vision may struggle to identify which calendar date is focused because the focus indicator has insufficient contrast (1.2:1 instead of the required 3:1). title: alert-light and table-light are not the same color code
body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [x] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

The CSS for .table-light and .alert-light are not the same, yet other colors are... .table-danger and .alert-danger are the same.

If you place a .table within a .alert and use the "light" color, there is a visual difference noticeable to human eye. 

.table-light is #fcfcfd
.alert-light is #f8f9fa


### Reduced test cases


To fix this, I just did a css override for the variable --bs-table-bg inside the .table-light class:
/* Fix bug between table-light and alert-light colors */
.table-light {
	--bs-table-bg: #fcfcfd;
}

### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Windows

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Firefox

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

5.3.8

author: TheDigitalOrchard

title: Bootstrap 6 defaulting to 14px instead of 16px for the $font-size-base
body:
Is there a reason in particular on why Bootstrap 6 is going to a different `$font-size-base` in `scss/_variables.scss` than the browser's default one which is `1rem` on all the browsers and operating systems I can think of.

I noticed this while looking at the v6 docs, the examples feel off on pretty much every component I've checked (badges, cards, inputs, buttons), they all look so small.

Tailwind, BS5, and BS4 all default to `1rem` (16px basically).

Is BS6 going to default to `1rem` moving forward or was this a temporary change that was left to `14px`?

Personally I believe, at least for the sake of accessibility, that the default `$font-size-base` should be set to `1rem`. I know this is configurable, but the defaults matter too.

 _Originally posted by @pricop in [#41740](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/pull/41740/changes/ac67589d418785b99b63e78f8062b64257a779fb#r2765052599)_

title: Popovers and tooltips should remain open when content is hovered
body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [x] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

Per https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG21/#content-on-hover-or-focus,

> If pointer hover can trigger the additional content, then the pointer can be moved over the additional content without the additional content disappearing;

I use popovers on certain links with `trigger: 'hover focus'`, which makes them covered under that guideline. Unfortunately there is no support for persisting a popover when the popover content receives hover. I have taken a crack at using manual triggers to achieve this, but given that it is explicitly called for in WCAG guidelines, it seems like this should at least be configurable if not default behavior.

The same non-compliant behavior applies to tooltips as well that are triggered by hover.

### Reduced test cases

No test case as this is easily demonstrable on all hover triggered popovers and tooltips (such as https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/components/tooltips/#directions)

### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Windows

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Chrome

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

5.3.8

title: Need for data version support in forms
body:
### Prerequisites - [x] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests - [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Proposal Forms are not static things: they do develop over time. When you get a new version of Android installed on your phone, it will show orange dots where new features were added. I do need a feature like that for forms: show where the user has different options since the last time that the user came to that form. In the usual set-up, the form gets filled with data from a database. When the form sees a change, for instance an extra option to a select, the database rules gets modified. But how does the user know that there is a new option? How does the user see that a new field appeared since the last time that form was visited. Examples: - the gender field first only had `male`, and `female`, hence some people were forced to pick one of these options, but where unhappy. Later, the database was made to support `other`. The form gets option `other`. For some reason, the user comes back to its profile but does not see the new and preferred option. - the ISP first supported login-names by self-chosen nickname, but wants people to force people into using an email-address as login. - the form informs the user that the `home address` information is removed due to privacy concerns. So: there is a large diversity in messages about the development of the form over time. Current solutions are really sloppy: some forms print a message somewhere for some time, for every one to see. Most changes happen without notice, even when they are worth to notice. See 'motivation' why I developed an abstraction for this in my application. It works conveniently, however it could be designed in a more generic way. It has similarities with `alert`. The `version` needs to be any sortable, for instance a date `YYYYMMDD`. Most of the implementation is simply "remove elements when no match" I think `visit` is a nice name for this feature: very distinctive. You must think "last visit of the user of the form". First, my idea for the introduction message of the form: ```html
Since Jan 1st, we are not allowed to register your address anymore. We have removed it from our administration.
Welcome back. loyal customer. You get an award.
``` In my implementation, I put these wrapped in fieldsets... which is a quite heavy. Now, an complex example showing some of the power. ```html
Do you need to buy a valentine present for your girl?
Presents are not optional anymore.
``` The basic implementation is simple: after form load, delete all dom elements with "visit-before" smaller than form."visit-version", and remove all with "visit-after" before form."visit-version". It would be nice to provide a generic way how to visualize the changes. The simplest is to inline them. But it might be useful to support a tooltip display over a question-mark icon, or a display within the form-leading "visit-explain". Main disadvantage: you throw away stuff which was first generated. For larger structures a waste of time. When this feature is accepted, then I can show more design for visualizations. Like orange dots. ### Motivation and context Of course you can implement above in the code which runs your template. However, this proposal makes the templates much, much cleaner. Form data versioning evolves from an ad-hoc trick into a first class citizen. Most forms speak with servers where the data is in a simple database. When the database changes, then the form changes in sync. I have two applications: one where the user data is signed. The server cannot modify the data to keep it in sync with the form's needs without breaking the signature. (Public distributed data store, like Solid) The other application uses a huge object store which makes instantaneous global form-data changes too expensive. So, in both cases I have to be able to handle many versions of possible source data: upgrade it to become compatible with the newest expectations on "some moment" in the future, often with need for user assistance.
author: markov2

title: [RFC] Architectural Audit: Potential memory leaks, XSS vectors, and SSR stability in 5.3.x
body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [x] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

While performing a deep dive into the 5.3.x source code for a custom integration, I’ve identified a few architectural patterns and edge cases that might lead to stability and security issues in specific environments (especially SPAs and SSR).

I’ve summarized the findings below:

1. Potential XSS via sanitizer.js
The sanitizeHtml function currently returns the result through innerHTML. While there is an allowList in place, using innerHTML as the primary injection vector is risky. If a developer misconfigures the SAFE_URL_PATTERN or the attribute filter, it opens a door for XSS attacks.

Recommendation: Consider more robust DOM manipulation or a stricter output sanitization layer to reduce the risk of bypasses.

2. Event Registry Memory Leaks (event-handler.js)
The eventRegistry keeps references to handlers tied to unique element IDs. In Single Page Applications (SPAs) where elements are frequently created and destroyed without an explicit call to dispose() or EventHandler.off(), these references persist. Over time, this leads to a significant memory footprint increase.

Recommendation: Perhaps implementing a more automated cleanup or utilizing WeakMap for registry storage could mitigate these leaks in dynamic environments.

3. Selector Engine Robustness (selector-engine.js)
Currently, find and findOne methods wrap querySelector directly without try-catch blocks. If an invalid CSS selector is passed through data-bs-target or href (e.g., unescaped special characters or :invalid(), the browser throws a DOMException, which halts the entire JS execution thread.

Recommendation: Wrapping queries in a try-catch to return null or an empty array would prevent the entire application from crashing due to a malformed attribute.

4. DOM Encapsulation Conflicts in modal.js
The _showElement method uses document.body.append(this._element). While this ensures the modal is on top, it forcefully moves the element in the DOM tree. This is a known "pain point" for React/Vue/Angular users, as it breaks the virtual DOM's encapsulation and can lead to runtime crashes during re-renders.

Recommendation: Acknowledging this is a legacy behavior, but exploring "Portal-like" logic or providing an option to toggle body-appending would greatly improve DX for modern framework users.

5. Config Merging and Validation Risks (config.js)
Shallow Merging: _mergeConfigObj uses the spread operator (...). This causes nested configuration objects to be overwritten rather than merged, which can lead to unexpected data loss.

RegExp Validation: Creating new RegExp(expectedTypes) on the fly without pre-validation is risky. A malformed expectedTypes string will throw a syntax error, preventing the component from initializing.

I’m happy to discuss these further or provide more specific examples if needed. Let me know if you’re open to PRs addressing any of these points!

### Reduced test cases

These are architectural findings based on a static analysis of the source code. A specific visual test case is not applicable for logic-based memory leaks and RegExp syntax risks, but the code locations are provided in the description.

### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Windows, macOS, Linux

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Chrome, Firefox, Microsoft Edge, Opera, Safari

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

v5.3.x (latest source as of Feb 16, 2026)

title: Consider speciesist language in documentation
body:
## Proposal

Add awareness of speciesist language — animal-derived idioms that normalize violence toward animals — to Bootstrap's documentation practices.

Bootstrap's documentation reaches millions of developers worldwide. As the most popular CSS framework, writing conventions here influence the entire front-end ecosystem.

## Common terms and alternatives

| Instead of | Use |
|---|---|
| "kill two birds with one stone" | "accomplish two things at once" |
| "beat a dead horse" | "belabor the point" |
| "monkey patch" | "runtime patch" |
| "guinea pig" | "test subject" |
| "canary" | "preview" |

These alternatives are more precise and more accessible to non-native English speakers — important for Bootstrap's global community.

## Precedent

- master→main (GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket)
- whitelist/blacklist→allowlist/blocklist (Google, Apple, IETF)
- master/slave→primary/replica (MySQL, PostgreSQL, Redis)
- [Inclusive Naming Initiative](https://inclusivenaming.org/)

## References

- Hagendorff et al. 2023, [DOI: 10.1007/s43681-023-00380-w](https://doi.org/10.1007/s43681-023-00380-w)
- [Open-Paws/speciesist-language-scanner](https://github.com/Open-Paws/speciesist-language-scanner)

This is about awareness for new writing, not a retroactive overhaul.

title: BS6 gap issues on alert component
body:
### Prerequisites - [x] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues - [x] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems - [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Describe the issue In BSv6, the `gap` property was introduced as part of the alert class: The issue with this is that now every element inside the `alert` container is now being applied a gap, and this applies to anchors too, leading to issues such as this: Where a gap is added on both sides of the `a` element. Furthermore, it will be applied to any other elements such as `strong`, `u`, or any other text decoration, which is very commonly used: The Documentation does not make it clear that in such cases, the contents of the alert should be inside of another container to prevent this, and I'm sure people won't catch this. I assume this was added to easily add gaps for the `icon` and `close` buttons, but the way I see it, those should be either using `ms-` utility or simply put in a grid with a gap instead. This change was done in December: https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/pull/41954/changes/97075b63b3d0c55ff4c8d9a56198b1a6a1843563 This issue is clearly visible at: https://v6-dev--twbs-bootstrap.netlify.app/docs/6.0/components/alert/#link-color So either the Documentation is going to need an update in the sense that when using the `alert` component, another parent element should be present for the text content (otherwise any text decoration will suffer from this), or the gap property goes away and we'll be using grid to do the spacing. Not sure which route you guys want to go. ### Reduced test cases ```
A simple accent alert with an example link. Give it a click if you like.
``` ### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on? Windows ### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on? Chrome ### What version of Bootstrap are you using? v6-dev
author: pricop

title: Bootstrap interactive components blocked by Chrome extension “VT4Browsers + Google TI”
body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [x] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

**Description**  
Some Bootstrap components (dropdowns, accordions, navbar-expand) stop working when the Chrome extension “VT4Browsers + Google TI” is installed and enabled.
This extension is now preinstalled by default in some Google Chrome distributions, which means the issue affects users even if they have never manually installed the extension.

The extension blocks the file **event-handler.js**, preventing Bootstrap’s event system from initializing. As a result, any component relying on event delegation becomes unusable.

**Steps to Reproduce**
Install Google Chrome with the preinstalled extension “VT4Browsers + Google TI”.
[VT4Browsers + Google TI](https://chromewebstore.google.com/detail/vt4browsers-+-google-ti/efbjojhplkelaegfbieplglfidafgoka)

Open any Bootstrap page containing interactive components (e.g., dropdowns, accordions, navbar toggler).
[bootswatch](https://bootswatch.com/flatly/)

Attempt to interact with the component.

**Expected Behavior**
Bootstrap components should behave normally:

Dropdowns open and close
Accordions expand and collapse
Navbar toggler expands the menu
All event-driven components work as documented

**Actual Behavior**
**Dropdowns do not work at all.** Clicking a dropdown toggle produces no visible action and the menu never opens.
**Accordions and navbar-expand toggles** can open, but they cannot close again. Once expanded, they remain stuck in the open state.
**No error messages** appear in the browser console. The console remains clean, which makes the issue harder to diagnose.
**However,** when inspecting the page via **Inspect → Elements → Event Listeners → Click**, all click listeners coming from **document : event-handler.js**. I looked into this in detail, but at no point did I see any explicit message or warning stating that these event listeners were “blocked by the extension.” The only observable behavior is that the listeners coming from event-handler.js simply do not execute.
If these blocked event listeners are **manually removed**, all Bootstrap components **immediately start working again** (dropdowns open, accordions and navbars can toggle normally).

**Additional Information**
**Disabling the extension immediately restores full functionality.**
**The issue occurs even on the official Bootstrap documentation website.**
**Since the extension is now preinstalled, many users may encounter this issue without understanding its cause.**
The extension “VT4Browsers + Google TI” is a security tool designed to scan URLs and detect potential malware or suspicious behavior. Despite this, when inspecting the Bootswatch Flatly theme page, the extension reports “No security vendors flagged this URL as malicious.” This indicates that the website and its resources are considered safe by all security checks.

**However,** even though the page is not flagged as malicious, the extension still blocks Bootstrap’s event-handler.js file. This suggests that the blocking behavior is likely a false positive or an overly aggressive rule within the extension, rather than an actual security threat.

**Environment**
Bootstrap version: I tried (5.3.8 & 5.3.4) [Bootstrap.bundle.min.js] & [Bootstrap.min.js + Poppers.js] 
Browser: Google Chrome (Version 144.0.7559.109  Official Build   64-bit)
OS: (Linux Lite)
Extensions: “VT4Browsers + Google TI” (preinstalled) : Version 5.0.0 Updated November 19, 2025

Google Chrome is preinstalled on Linux Lite and i find the Bug by opening Bootstwath and my Current Development using Bootstrap 5.3.8 in Brave (Without "VT4Browsers + Google TI").

**Possible Cause**
The extension blocks Bootstrap’s event-handling script, preventing event delegation from being registered.

Request
Could the Bootstrap team confirm whether this is a known compatibility issue and whether there are recommended workarounds or adjustments to avoid the extension blocking event-handler.js?

### Reduced test cases

[[Bootswatch](https://bootswatch.com/)](https://bootswatch.com/flatly/) 
**use** : bootstrap.bundle.min.js v5.3.8 



### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Linux

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Chrome

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

v5.3.8

title: Ascending check does not allow equal $container-max-widths even when desired
body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [x] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

In our app, we want the container max width to not grow from the `md` breakpoint onwards. However, this triggers the following warning from Bootstrap's `-assert-ascending()`:

```
WARNING: Invalid value for $container-max-widths: This map must be in ascending order, but key 'xxl' has value 38.5rem which isn't greater than 38.5rem, the value of the previous key 'xl' !
```

As there is nothing wrong with not growing the max-width at some point, I don't think this should be warning. Checking that the values are monotonically increasing, but possibly unchanging should be enough to prevent accidental issues.

### Reduced test cases

The following should be sufficient to reproduce the issue:

```scss
$container-max-widths: (
  sm: 100%,
  md: 616px,
  lg: 616px,
  xl: 616px,
  xxl: 616px,
);

@import "bootstrap";
```

### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

macOS

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Firefox

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

5.3.8

title: Replace RTLCSS with logical properties and no extra transform/build step
body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests
- [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Proposal

Currently RTLCSS is required:
https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/getting-started/rtl/#approach

This means for our own custom SASS builds of bootstrap, we also need to add the RTLCSS post-build step to our processes, which is clunky and feels antiquated in 2026 since all modern browsers support logical properties directly without needing a separate `*.rtl.css` file
* https://caniuse.com/css-logical-props


### Motivation and context

I think the reliance on RTLCSS is a relic leftover from when Bootstrap had to support RTL but browsers didn't have good standard support for it.
author: NateRadebaugh

title: `.input-group` incorrectly applies full border radius to `<select.form-select>` when preceded by `.input-group-text` label (ASP.NET rendered select case)
body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [x] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

When using `.input-group` with a left-side `.input-group-text` label followed by a `<select class="form-select">`, Bootstrap sometimes treats the `<select>` as a standalone control instead of part of the input group.

As a result:

- full border-radius is applied on all 4 corners
- expected right-side-only rounding is not applied
- visual alignment breaks with the label addon

This happens even when:

- `.form-select` is explicitly applied
- markup structure matches Bootstrap documentation expectations
- element is rendered as a native `<select>` in final HTML

Temporary workaround:
```
$(document).ready(function () {
  $('#DropDownList1').removeClass('dg-single');
});
```

### Reduced test cases

**CodePen:** [https://codepen.io/Sherif-Riad/pen/KwgyxRa](https://codepen.io/Sherif-Riad/pen/KwgyxRa)

The issue reproduces identically in static HTML and ASP.NET WebForms rendered `<select class="form-select">` controls. In WebForms environments, the problem occurs consistently even when `.form-select` is applied server-side via `CssClass`.

### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Windows

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Chrome

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

v5.3.8
author: Sherif-Riad

title: Streamline local documentation setup and clarify v6 development roadmap
body:
## Description
The current `README.md` provides a solid overview of Bootstrap 5, but as the ecosystem moves toward more modern tooling and the next major version of Bootstrap, certain sections of the README would benefit from a "Quality of Life" update to improve the developer onboarding experience.

## Proposed Improvements
* **Bun Integration in Docs Setup:**
      - The "Quick Start" section already includes bun add, but the "Running documentation locally" section still exclusively references `npm`.
      - Task: Update the documentation setup instructions to include Bun equivalents (e.g., bun install and bun run docs-serve) to maintain    consistency across the README.
      
* **Roadmap Transparency (v6 Development):**
      - The README mentions that the default branch is for "Bootstrap 5 release," but there is significant community interest in Bootstrap 6.
      - Task: Add a small "Roadmap" or "Next Major Version" section (or a link to a GitHub Project board) to clarify where the v6 development is happening, similar to how the v4-dev branch is currently linked.

* **Astro Dependency Clarity:**
     - Step 2 of "Running documentation locally" mentions running `npm run test` to rebuild assets. For many documentation contributors, rebuilding the entire distribution suite is an unnecessary bottleneck.
     - Task: Clarify if there is a more "lean" command for users who only want to contribute to the docs content without rebuilding the full JS/CSS distribution.

* **Visual Status Refresh:**
    - Some of the badges (like Coveralls and OSSF Scorecard) are essential for a project of this scale.
    - Task: Re-organize the "Status" badges into a grid to reduce the vertical "wall of badges" and make the "Build Status" and "Version" more prominent.

## Tasks
- [ ] Align Quick Start installation commands with Local Docs setup commands (Add Bun).
- [ ] Add a prominent link to the Bootstrap 6 development status.
- [ ] Simplify the Documentation local run instructions.
- [ ] Refactor the Status badge section for better scannability.

## Why this is needed
1. **Onboarding Speed:** New contributors using Bun or Astro should have a seamless experience that matches the modern web standards Bootstrap promotes.
2. **Community Expectations:** Clearly defining the line between v5 maintenance and v6 innovation reduces `"Where is v6?"` inquiries in Discussions.
3. **Readability:** As one of the most starred repos on GitHub, the README should be the gold standard for "Apple-level" clean documentation.

author: jitendrawebtech

title: Missing aria-pressed="false" at toggle buttons initialization (button plugin)
body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [x] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

As indicated in [MDN aria-pressed documentation](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Accessibility/ARIA/Reference/Attributes/aria-pressed) and in the [aria toggle button pattern documentation](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/button/), a toggle button should always have an `aria-pressed` attribute indicating its toggle nature, even when the value is `false` (because default value is `indeterminate` and not `false`).

In Bootstrap's doc, at the initialization, `aria-pressed="false"` is never set, which means assistive technologies will not be able to detect that this is a toggle button, and therefore will not announce it. 

### Reduced test cases

See documentation here https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/components/buttons/#button-plugin and here https://v6-dev--twbs-bootstrap.netlify.app/docs/6.0/components/buttons/#button-plugin

### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Windows

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Chrome

v5.3, v6-dev
